input.prompt: Describe what this screenshot shows. Focus on the swim lanes/columns, which tasks are in which columns, and especially any tasks that appear to have mismatched status vs swim lane position.

content.content:
I appreciate your request, but the content you've provided appears to be raw PNG image data (binary encoded), not readable text or a viewable screenshot. The data shows PNG file headers and compressed image information, but I cannot decode or interpret the actual visual content.

To help you analyze a screenshot showing swim lanes, task positions, and status mismatches, please:

1. **Share the image directly** through a platform that displays it visually, or
2. **Describe what you see** in the screenshot, or
3. **Provide a text-based summary** of the columns and tasks

Once you share the visual information in a readable format, I'd be happy to help identify which tasks are in which swim lanes and flag any status-position inconsistencies you've noticed.

    "description": "## Problem\nWhen a user is invited to a team, the email notification requires them to log in and navigate to Settings to manually accept the invitation. This is a poor UX — users should be able to accept directly from the email.\n\n## Requirements\n- Team invitation emails should include a prominent \"Accept Invitation\" CTA button\n- Clicking the link should automatically accept the invitation and add the user to the team\n- The link should contain a signed/tokenized URL (e.g. `/api/invites/team/accept?token=XXXX`) so acceptance is secure and one-click\n- For existing users: accept → redirect to the project board\n- For new users: accept → redirect to signup (with invite code pre-filled), then auto-accept after account creation\n- Expired or already-accepted links should show a clear message\n\n## Implementation Notes\n- Backend: Add a `team_invite_token` column or use a signed JWT with team_member_id + expiry\n- Backend: New endpoint `GET /api/invites/team/accept?token=XXX` that validates token, updates team_members status to accepted, redirects to frontend\n- Frontend: Handle the redirect route, show success/error state\n- Email template: Update `SendProjectInvitation` and `SendProjectInvitationNewUser` to use the accept URL instead of a generic login link",

  {
    "id": 7,
    "project_id": 1,
    "task_number": 7,
    "title": "Image library with alt-name search and cross-project sharing",
    "description": "## Requirements\n\n### Image Alt-Name\n- Each image attachment should have an editable alt-name/description field\n- Alt-name is searchable and shown in previews\n\n### Image Upload & Reuse\n- Users can upload new images or reuse existing ones from the shared library\n- When attaching an image to a task, show a picker with:\n  - Upload new file option\n  - Search existing images by alt-name with preview thumbnails\n  - Grid/list view of available images\n\n### Shared Image Library\n- All images uploaded by the user across any project are visible to them\n- Images from other team members in shared projects are also visible\n- Members with NO shared project relationship cannot see each others images\n- Image visibility scoping: user_images UNION (images from members who share at least one project with the current user)\n\n### Implementation Approach\n- Add `alt_name` column to task_attachments table\n- Create a new `/api/images` endpoint that returns all accessible images for the current user\n- Add search endpoint: `/api/images?q=search_term` filters by alt_name\n- Frontend: Image picker component with search, preview grid, and upload\n- Frontend: Inline alt-name editing on attached images",
    "status": "done",
    "swim_lane_id": 3,
    "swim_lane_name": "Done",
    "priority": "medium",
    "created_at": "2026-02-13T04:20:26Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-02-14T01:59:04Z"
  },
  {
    "id": 6,
    "project_id": 1,
    "task_number": 6,
    "title": "Task modal popup view (like GitHub Issues)",
    "description": "## Overview\nOpen tasks as a full-screen modal overlay from the kanban board, instead of navigating to a separate page. Keep the separate page working for direct URLs.\n\n## Requirements\n\n### Modal Behavior\n- Clicking a task card on the kanban board opens a modal overlay\n- Modal shows full task detail (same content as TaskDetail page)\n- Board remains visible behind the semi-transparent backdrop\n- Close via: X button, clicking backdrop, Escape key, or browser back\n\n### URL Integration\n- URL updates to `/app/projects/:id/tasks/:taskId` when modal opens\n- Browser back button closes modal (returns to board URL)\n- Direct navigation to `/app/projects/:id/tasks/:taskId` renders standalone page\n- Shareable URLs work in both contexts\n\n### Implementation\n- Use React Router background location pattern:\n  - `navigate(taskUrl, { state: { backgroundLocation: location } })`\n  - In App/Dashboard router, check for `location.state?.backgroundLocation`\n  - If present, render board at background location + modal overlay at current location\n  - If not present, render TaskDetail as standalone page\n- Smooth fade-in/slide-up animation on open\n- Modal should be scrollable if content overflows\n- Max width ~900px, centered, with rounded corners\n\n### Technical Notes\n- TaskDetail component must work in both modal and standalone mode\n- Pass `isModal` prop to control layout (no page-level padding in modal mode)\n- Inline editing (task #5) should work inside the modal too",
    "status": "done",
    "swim_lane_id": 3,
    "swim_lane_name": "Done",
    "priority": "medium",
    "created_at": "2026-02-13T03:56:12Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-02-14T01:59:04Z"
  },
  {
    "id": 5,
    "project_id": 1,
    "task_number": 5,
    "title": "Inline field editing on task detail page (remove Edit button)",
    "description": "## Overview\nModernize the task detail page so every field is directly editable by clicking on it, like GitHub Issues. Remove the global \"Edit\" button.\n\n## Requirements\n\n### Inline Editable Fields\n- **Title**: Click to edit inline, save on blur/Enter, cancel on Escape\n- **Status/Swim Lane**: Click to show dropdown, select to save immediately\n- **Priority**: Click to show dropdown, select to save\n- **Sprint**: Click to show dropdown, select to save\n- **Assignee**: Click to show dropdown/input, save on select\n- **Due Date**: Click to show date picker, save on select\n- **Estimated/Actual Hours**: Click to show number input, save on blur/Enter\n- **Description**: Show small edit (pencil) icon on hover, click opens markdown editor, save/cancel buttons\n\n### Behavior\n- Each field saves independently via PATCH `/api/tasks/:id`\n- Show subtle hover state (dashed border or pencil icon) to indicate editability\n- Optimistic update: show new value immediately, revert + toast on error\n- Brief checkmark or flash animation on successful save\n- No global Edit/Save/Cancel buttons — remove them entirely\n\n### Components to Build\n1. `InlineText` — click-to-edit single-line text\n2. `InlineSelect` — click-to-edit dropdown\n3. `InlineDatePicker` — click-to-edit date\n4. `InlineNumber` — click-to-edit number with step\n5. `InlineMarkdown` — edit icon triggers markdown editor with preview\n\n### Technical Notes\n- No backend changes needed (PATCH already supports partial updates)\n- Reuse existing `apiClient.updateTask()` for each field save\n- Must work in both standalone page and future modal view",
    "status": "done",
    "swim_lane_id": 3,
    "swim_lane_name": "Done",
    "priority": "high",
    "created_at": "2026-02-13T03:55:49Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-02-14T01:59:04Z"
  },
  {
    "id": 4,
    "project_id": 1,
    "task_number": 4,
    "title": "Add Cloudinary settings and file upload support",
    "description": "## Overview\nAdd Cloudinary API Key and Secret fields to user Settings page, then enable file uploads (images, videos, PDFs) on tasks.\n\n## Requirements\n\n### Settings UI\n- Add \"Cloudinary\" section to Settings page\n- Fields: Cloud Name, API Key, API Secret (masked)\n- Save/update/delete credentials\n- Show connection status (valid/invalid) after saving\n\n### File Upload\n- Allow uploading images, videos, and PDFs to tasks (as attachments)\n- Default size limit: 10 MB per file (user-configurable in settings)\n- Upload via server-side proxy using the uploading user's Cloudinary credentials\n- If no credentials configured, show prompt linking to Settings\n\n### Multi-User Quota\n- Track bytes uploaded per user per project in `file_uploads` table\n- Multiple users in same project each use their own Cloudinary account\n- Store file metadata (URL, size, type, cloudinary_public_id, user_id) in DB\n- Files visible to all project members regardless of who uploaded\n\n### Storage Visualization\n- Show storage usage per user per project in Project Settings\n- Breakdown by file type (images/videos/documents)\n- Progress bars showing relative usage\n\n### Backend\n1. DB Migration: `cloudinary_credentials` table + `file_uploads` table\n2. Endpoints: CRUD credentials, upload file, get usage stats\n3. Encrypt API Secret with AES-256, never return in plaintext\n4. Validate credentials on save by making test Cloudinary API call",
    "status": "done",
    "swim_lane_id": 3,
    "swim_lane_name": "Done",
    "priority": "high",
    "created_at": "2026-02-13T03:54:57Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-02-14T01:59:04Z"
  },
  {
    "id": 3,
    "project_id": 1,
    "task_number": 3,
    "title": "Modernize task UI: inline editing and modal view",
    "description": "## Overview\nModernize the task interaction UX to match GitHub's issue experience — inline field editing and modal-based task view.\n\n## Requirements\n\n### Inline Field Editing (like GitHub Issues)\n- Remove the \"Edit\" button paradigm — each field should be directly editable\n- Click on title → inline text input, save on blur/Enter, cancel on Escape\n- Click on description → inline markdown editor with preview\n- Click on status/priority/assignee → inline dropdown selector\n- Click on due date → inline date picker\n- Click on sprint → inline dropdown\n- Each field saves independently via PATCH API call\n- Show subtle hover indicators (pencil icon or border change) on editable fields\n- Optimistic updates with error rollback\n\n### Task Modal View (like GitHub Issues)\n- Clicking a task on the kanban board opens a full-screen modal overlay\n- Modal shows complete task detail (same layout as current TaskDetail page)\n- URL updates to `/app/projects/:id/tasks/:taskId` when modal opens\n- Browser back button closes modal and returns to board\n- Direct navigation to `/app/projects/:id/tasks/:taskId` still works as standalone page\n- Modal has close button (X) and clicking backdrop also closes\n- Smooth open/close animations\n\n### Implementation\n1. **Inline Edit Components**: Create `InlineText`, `InlineTextarea`, `InlineSelect`, `InlineDatePicker` reusable components\n2. **Task Modal**: Create `TaskModal` component wrapping task detail content\n3. **Router Integration**: Use React Router's modal route pattern (outlet context)\n4. **API**: Already supports PATCH with partial updates — no backend changes needed\n5. **Frontend**: Update ProjectDetail to open modal on task click, update TaskDetail to support both modal and standalone modes",
    "status": "done",
    "swim_lane_id": 3,
    "swim_lane_name": "Done",
    "priority": "high",
    "created_at": "2026-02-13T03:40:29Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-02-14T01:59:04Z"
  },
  {
    "id": 2,
    "project_id": 1,
    "task_number": 2,
    "title": "Cloudinary integration for file uploads with quota management",
    "description": "## Overview\nEnable file uploads (images, videos, PDFs) via Cloudinary with per-user API key management and quota tracking.\n\n## Requirements\n\n### User Settings\n- Add Cloudinary API Key and API Secret fields to user/project settings\n- Store encrypted, never expose secret in API responses\n- Multiple users in same project can each add their own keys\n\n### Upload Flow\n- Support image, video, and PDF uploads on tasks (description, comments, attachments)\n- Use the uploading user's Cloudinary credentials\n- If user has no key configured, show prompt to add one in settings\n\n### Quota Management\n- Track bytes uploaded per user per project\n- When multiple users share a project, split storage across their individual Cloudinary accounts\n- Store file metadata (URL, size, user_id, cloudinary_public_id) in our DB\n- Files uploaded by User A use User A's Cloudinary quota, but are visible to all project members\n\n### Visualization\n- Dashboard showing storage usage per user per project\n- Progress bars or charts showing quota consumption\n- Breakdown by file type (images vs videos vs documents)\n\n### Implementation\n1. **DB Migration**: `cloudinary_credentials` table, `file_uploads` table with size/user tracking\n2. **Backend**: Upload endpoint proxying to Cloudinary, quota tracking middleware\n3. **API**: CRUD for credentials, upload endpoint, usage stats endpoint\n4. **Frontend**: Settings UI for API keys, upload widget, usage dashboard",
    "status": "done",
    "swim_lane_id": 3,
    "swim_lane_name": "Done",
    "priority": "high",
    "created_at": "2026-02-13T03:40:15Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-02-14T01:59:04Z"
  },
  {
    "id": 1,
    "project_id": 1,
    "task_number": 1,
    "title": "Sequential task numbers per project",
    "description": "## Problem\nCurrently task IDs are global auto-increment integers. Users see task #47 in a project that only has 3 tasks, which is confusing.\n\n## Solution\nAdd a `task_number` field that auto-increments per project, starting from 1. Each project maintains its own counter.\n\n### Requirements\n- Add `task_number` column to tasks table (integer, not null)\n- Auto-assign next number on task creation per project\n- Display as `#1`, `#2`, `#3` in the UI (project board, task detail, etc.)\n- Task number is immutable once assigned\n- Unique constraint: `(project_id, task_number)`\n\n### Implementation\n1. **DB Migration**: Add `task_number` column + unique index\n2. **Backend**: Calculate next number on INSERT using `SELECT MAX(task_number) + 1 FROM tasks WHERE project_id = ?`\n3. **API**: Return `task_number` in task responses\n4. **Frontend**: Display `#N` prefix on task cards, detail page, and search results",
    "status": "done",
    "swim_lane_id": 3,
    "swim_lane_name": "Done",
    "priority": "high",
    "created_at": "2026-02-13T03:40:03Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-02-14T01:59:04Z"
  }
]

This is a poor UX — users should be able to accept directly from the email.\n\n## Requirements\n- Team invitation emails should include a prominent \"Accept Invitation\" CTA button\n- Clicking the link should automatically accept the invitation and add the user to the team\n- The link should contain a signed/tokenized URL (e.g. `/api/invites/team/accept?token=XXXX`) so acceptance is secure and one-click\n- For existing users: accept → redirect to the project board\n- For new users: accept → redirect to signup (with invite code pre-filled), then auto-accept after account creation\n- Expired or already-accepted links should show a clear message\n\n## Implementation Notes\n- Backend: Add a `team_invite_token` column or use a signed JWT with team_member_id + expiry\n- Backend: New endpoint `GET /api/invites/team/accept?token=XXX` that validates token, updates team_members status to accepted, redirects to frontend\n- Frontend: Handle the redirect route, show success/error state\n- Email template: Update `SendProjectInvitation` and `SendProjectInvitationNewUser` to use the accept URL instead of a generic login link",

    "title": "Image library with alt-name search and cross-project sharing",
    "description": "## Requirements\n\n### Image Alt-Name\n- Each image attachment should have an editable alt-name/description field\n- Alt-name is searchable and shown in previews\n\n### Image Upload & Reuse\n- Users can upload new images or reuse existing ones from the shared library\n- When attaching an image to a task, show a picker with:\n  - Upload new file option\n  - Search existing images by alt-name with preview thumbnails\n  - Grid/list view of available images\n\n### Shared Image Library\n- All images uploaded by the user across any project are visible to them\n- Images from other team members in shared projects are also visible\n- Members with NO shared project relationship cannot see each others images\n- Image visibility scoping: user_images UNION (images from members who share at least one project with the current user)\n\n### Implementation Approach\n- Add `alt_name` column to task_attachments table\n- Create a new `/api/images` endpoint that returns all accessible images for the current user\n- Add search endpoint: `/api/images?q=search_term` filters by alt_name\n- Frontend: Image picker component with search, preview grid, and upload\n- Frontend: Inline alt-name editing on attached images",

    "title": "Task modal popup view (like GitHub Issues)",
    "description": "## Overview\nOpen tasks as a full-screen modal overlay from the kanban board, instead of navigating to a separate page. Keep the separate page working for direct URLs.\n\n## Requirements\n\n### Modal Behavior\n- Clicking a task card on the kanban board opens a modal overlay\n- Modal shows full task detail (same content as TaskDetail page)\n- Board remains visible behind the semi-transparent backdrop\n- Close via: X button, clicking backdrop, Escape key, or browser back\n\n### URL Integration\n- URL updates to `/app/projects/:id/tasks/:taskId` when modal opens\n- Browser back button closes modal (returns to board URL)\n- Direct navigation to `/app/projects/:id/tasks/:taskId` renders standalone page\n- Shareable URLs work in both contexts\n\n### Implementation\n- Use React Router background location pattern:\n  - `navigate(taskUrl, { state: { backgroundLocation: location } })`\n  - In App/Dashboard router, check for `location.state?.backgroundLocation`\n  - If present, render board at background location + modal overlay at current location\n  - If not present, render TaskDetail as standalone page\n- Smooth fade-in/slide-up animation on open\n- Modal should be scrollable if content overflows\n- Max width ~900px, centered, with rounded corners\n\n### Technical Notes\n- TaskDetail component must work in both modal and standalone mode\n- Pass `isModal` prop to control layout (no page-level padding in modal mode)\n- Inline editing (task #5) should work inside the modal too",
    "status": "done",
    "swim_lane_id": 3,
    "swim_lane_name": "Done",
    "priority": "medium",
    "created_at": "2026-02-13T03:56:12Z",

  {
    "id": 5,
    "project_id": 1,
    "task_number": 5,
    "title": "Inline field editing on task detail page (remove Edit button)",
    "description": "## Overview\nModernize the task detail page so every field is directly editable by clicking on it, like GitHub Issues. Remove the global \"Edit\" button.\n\n## Requirements\n\n### Inline Editable Fields\n- **Title**: Click to edit inline, save on blur/Enter, cancel on Escape\n- **Status/Swim Lane**: Click to show dropdown, select to save immediately\n- **Priority**: Click to show dropdown, select to save\n- **Sprint**: Click to show dropdown, select to save\n- **Assignee**: Click to show dropdown/input, save on select\n- **Due Date**: Click to show date picker, save on select\n- **Estimated/Actual Hours**: Click to show number input, save on blur/Enter\n- **Description**: Show small edit (pencil) icon on hover, click opens markdown editor, save/cancel buttons\n\n### Behavior\n- Each field saves independently via PATCH `/api/tasks/:id`\n- Show subtle hover state (dashed border or pencil icon) to indicate editability\n- Optimistic update: show new value immediately, revert + toast on error\n- Brief checkmark or flash animation on successful save\n- No global Edit/Save/Cancel buttons — remove them entirely\n\n### Components to Build\n1. `InlineText` — click-to-edit single-line text\n2. `InlineSelect` — click-to-edit dropdown\n3. `InlineDatePicker` — click-to-edit date\n4. `InlineNumber` — click-to-edit number with step\n5. `InlineMarkdown` — edit icon triggers markdown editor with preview\n\n### Technical Notes\n- No backend changes needed (PATCH already supports partial updates)\n- Reuse existing `apiClient.updateTask()` for each field save\n- Must work in both standalone page and future modal view",
    "status": "done",
    "swim_lane_id": 3,
    "swim_lane_name": "Done",
    "priority": "high",
    "created_at": "2026-02-13T03:55:49Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-02-14T01:59:04Z"

    "task_number": 4,
    "title": "Add Cloudinary settings and file upload support",
    "description": "## Overview\nAdd Cloudinary API Key and Secret fields to user Settings page, then enable file uploads (images, videos, PDFs) on tasks.\n\n## Requirements\n\n### Settings UI\n- Add \"Cloudinary\" section to Settings page\n- Fields: Cloud Name, API Key, API Secret (masked)\n- Save/update/delete credentials\n- Show connection status (valid/invalid) after saving\n\n### File Upload\n- Allow uploading images, videos, and PDFs to tasks (as attachments)\n- Default size limit: 10 MB per file (user-configurable in settings)\n- Upload via server-side proxy using the uploading user's Cloudinary credentials\n- If no credentials configured, show prompt linking to Settings\n\n### Multi-User Quota\n- Track bytes uploaded per user per project in `file_uploads` table\n- Multiple users in same project each use their own Cloudinary account\n- Store file metadata (URL, size, type, cloudinary_public_id, user_id) in DB\n- Files visible to all project members regardless of who uploaded\n\n### Storage Visualization\n- Show storage usage per user per project in Project Settings\n- Breakdown by file type (images/videos/documents)\n- Progress bars showing relative usage\n\n### Backend\n1. DB Migration: `cloudinary_credentials` table + `file_uploads` table\n2. Endpoints: CRUD credentials, upload file, get usage stats\n3. Encrypt API Secret with AES-256, never return in plaintext\n4. Validate credentials on save by making test Cloudinary API call",
    "status": "done",
    "swim_lane_id": 3,
    "swim_lane_name": "Done",
    "priority": "high",
    "created_at": "2026-02-13T03:54:57Z",

  {
    "id": 3,
    "project_id": 1,
    "task_number": 3,
    "title": "Modernize task UI: inline editing and modal view",
    "description": "## Overview\nModernize the task interaction UX to match GitHub's issue experience — inline field editing and modal-based task view.\n\n## Requirements\n\n### Inline Field Editing (like GitHub Issues)\n- Remove the \"Edit\" button paradigm — each field should be directly editable\n- Click on title → inline text input, save on blur/Enter, cancel on Escape\n- Click on description → inline markdown editor with preview\n- Click on status/priority/assignee → inline dropdown selector\n- Click on due date → inline date picker\n- Click on sprint → inline dropdown\n- Each field saves independently via PATCH API call\n- Show subtle hover indicators (pencil icon or border change) on editable fields\n- Optimistic updates with error rollback\n\n### Task Modal View (like GitHub Issues)\n- Clicking a task on the kanban board opens a full-screen modal overlay\n- Modal shows complete task detail (same layout as current TaskDetail page)\n- URL updates to `/app/projects/:id/tasks/:taskId` when modal opens\n- Browser back button closes modal and returns to board\n- Direct navigation to `/app/projects/:id/tasks/:taskId` still works as standalone page\n- Modal has close button (X) and clicking backdrop also closes\n- Smooth open/close animations\n\n### Implementation\n1. **Inline Edit Components**: Create `InlineText`, `InlineTextarea`, `InlineSelect`, `InlineDatePicker` reusable components\n2. **Task Modal**: Create `TaskModal` component wrapping task detail content\n3. **Router Integration**: Use React Router's modal route pattern (outlet context)\n4. **API**: Already supports PATCH with partial updates — no backend changes needed\n5. **Frontend**: Update ProjectDetail to open modal on task click, update TaskDetail to support both modal and standalone modes",
    "status": "done",
    "swim_lane_id": 3,
    "swim_lane_name": "Done",
    "priority": "high",
    "created_at": "2026-02-13T03:40:29Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-02-14T01:59:04Z"
  },
  {
    "id": 2,
    "project_id": 1,
    "task_number": 2,
    "title": "Cloudinary integration for file uploads with quota management",
    "description": "## Overview\nEnable file uploads (images, videos, PDFs) via Cloudinary with per-user API key management and quota tracking.\n\n## Requirements\n\n### User Settings\n- Add Cloudinary API Key and API Secret fields to user/project settings\n- Store encrypted, never expose secret in API responses\n- Multiple users in same project can each add their own keys\n\n### Upload Flow\n- Support image, video, and PDF uploads on tasks (description, comments, attachments)\n- Use the uploading user's Cloudinary credentials\n- If user has no key configured, show prompt to add one in settings\n\n### Quota Management\n- Track bytes uploaded per user per project\n- When multiple users share a project, split storage across their individual Cloudinary accounts\n- Store file metadata (URL, size, user_id, cloudinary_public_id) in our DB\n- Files uploaded by User A use User A's Cloudinary quota, but are visible to all project members\n\n### Visualization\n- Dashboard showing storage usage per user per project\n- Progress bars or charts showing quota consumption\n- Breakdown by file type (images vs videos vs documents)\n\n### Implementation\n1. **DB Migration**: `cloudinary_credentials` table, `file_uploads` table with size/user tracking\n2. **Backend**: Upload endpoint proxying to Cloudinary, quota tracking middleware\n3. **API**: CRUD for credentials, upload endpoint, usage stats endpoint\n4. **Frontend**: Settings UI for API keys, upload widget, usage dashboard",
    "status": "done",
    "swim_lane_id": 3,
    "swim_lane_name": "Done",
    "priority": "high",
    "created_at": "2026-02-13T03:40:15Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-02-14T01:59:04Z"
  },
  {
    "id": 1,
    "project_id": 1,
    "task_number": 1,
    "title": "Sequential task numbers per project",
    "description": "## Problem\nCurrently task IDs are global auto-increment integers. Users see task #47 in a project that only has 3 tasks, which is confusing.\n\n## Solution\nAdd a `task_number` field that auto-increments per project, starting from 1. Each project maintains its own counter.\n\n### Requirements\n- Add `task_number` column to tasks table (integer, not null)\n- Auto-assign next number on task creation per project\n- Display as `#1`, `#2`, `#3` in the UI (project board, task detail, etc.)\n- Task number is immutable once assigned\n- Unique constraint: `(project_id, task_number)`\n\n### Implementation\n1. **DB Migration**: Add `task_number` column + unique index\n2. **Backend**: Calculate next number on INSERT using `SELECT MAX(task_number) + 1 FROM tasks WHERE project_id = ?`\n3. **API**: Return `task_number` in task responses\n4. **Frontend**: Display `#N` prefix on task cards, detail page, and search results",
    "status": "done",
    "swim_lane_id": 3,
    "swim_lane_name": "Done",
    "priority": "high",
    "created_at": "2026-02-13T03:40:03Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-02-14T01:59:04Z"
  }
]

Looking at the task list, all tasks currently have consistent swim_lane_id and status:
- Tasks with status "todo" have swim_lane_id=1 (To Do)
- Tasks with status "done" have swim_lane_id=3 (Done)

But the user says "some of these are done but in swim lane to do". Let me look at the screenshot. The WebFetch couldn't parse the image. Let me look at specific tasks more carefully.

Wait - tasks 15, 16, 17 have status "todo" and swim_lane_id=1. But the user says these are "done". Let me look at the context:


- Task 15: "Fix Admin Page" - status todo, swim_lane To Do
- Task 16: "Add Brevo" - status todo, swim_lane To Do  
- Task 17: "Send emails" - status todo, swim_lane To Do
- Task 18: "One-click team invite" - status todo, swim_lane To Do

The user is saying these tasks (15, 16, 17, 18) should be marked as done because they've already been completed. Let me check the recent commits to verify which ones are actually finished.

Looking at the commit history, I can see that Brevo was added, the admin page was redesigned, email sending was implemented, and one-click team invite acceptance from email was added. So all four tasks have been completed in the code, but their status in the task board is still showing as todo. The issue is that the task statuses aren't syncing with the actual implementation state, and I need to figure out why that disconnect exists and fix it. Looking at the API response more carefully, tasks marked as "done" are correctly assigned to swim_lane_id=3 (the Done column), so the issue must be that tasks 15-18 which represent completed work (Brevo, admin fix, emails, invite) still have status="todo" and need their status updated to reflect they're actually finished.

content.text: I can see the data from the API. Let me check the code to understand the swim lane/status sync issue. I'll also check the screenshot by downloading it.

input.prompt:
Explore the SprintSpark codebase thoroughly to understand the swim lane / status synchronization logic. The issue is that tasks can end up with mismatched status and swim lane (e.g., status="done" but swim_lane_id pointing to "To Do" column, or vice versa).

Look at:
1. Backend API handlers for task updates (PATCH /api/tasks/:id) - how does it handle swim_lane_id and status fields when one is updated?
2. Backend API handlers for task creation - does it sync status with swim_lane?
3. Frontend code that updates tasks (drag and drop on kanban board, status dropdown changes, swim lane changes)
4. The database schema for tasks and swim_lanes tables
5. The recent commit "feat: fix swim lane/status sync" to see what was already fixed

Focus on finding any code paths where status and swim_lane_id can get out of sync. Be very thorough - check all update paths including drag-and-drop, inline editing, task creation, and API endpoints.

1→package api
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"database/sql"
     6→	"encoding/json"
     7→	"net/http"
     8→	"strconv"
     9→	"time"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/go-chi/chi/v5"
    12→)
    13→
    14→type Task struct {
    15→	ID             int64     `json:"id"`
    16→	ProjectID      int64     `json:"project_id"`
    17→	TaskNumber     int64     `json:"task_number"`
    18→	Title          string    `json:"title"`
    19→	Description    *string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
    20→	Status         string    `json:"status"`
    21→	SwimLaneID     *int64    `json:"swim_lane_id,omitempty"`
    22→	SwimLaneName   *string   `json:"swim_lane_name,omitempty"`
    23→	DueDate        *string   `json:"due_date,omitempty"`
    24→	SprintID       *int64    `json:"sprint_id,omitempty"`
    25→	SprintName     *string   `json:"sprint_name,omitempty"`
    26→	Priority       string    `json:"priority"`
    27→	AssigneeID     *int64    `json:"assignee_id,omitempty"`
    28→	AssigneeName   *string   `json:"assignee_name,omitempty"`
    29→	EstimatedHours *float64  `json:"estimated_hours,omitempty"`
    30→	ActualHours    *float64  `json:"actual_hours,omitempty"`
    31→	Tags           []Tag     `json:"tags,omitempty"`
    32→	CreatedAt      time.Time `json:"created_at"`
    33→	UpdatedAt      time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
    34→}
    35→
    36→type CreateTaskRequest struct {
    37→	Title          string   `json:"title"`
    38→	Description    *string  `json:"description,omitempty"`
    39→	Status         *string  `json:"status,omitempty"`
    40→	SwimLaneID     *int64   `json:"swim_lane_id,omitempty"`
    41→	DueDate        *string  `json:"due_date,omitempty"`
    42→	SprintID       *int64   `json:"sprint_id,omitempty"`
    43→	Priority       *string  `json:"priority,omitempty"`
    44→	AssigneeID     *int64   `json:"assignee_id,omitempty"`
    45→	EstimatedHours *float64 `json:"estimated_hours,omitempty"`
    46→	ActualHours    *float64 `json:"actual_hours,omitempty"`
    47→	TagIDs         []int64  `json:"tag_ids,omitempty"`
    48→}
    49→
    50→type UpdateTaskRequest struct {
    51→	Title          *string  `json:"title,omitempty"`
    52→	Description    *string  `json:"description,omitempty"`
    53→	Status         *string  `json:"status,omitempty"`
    54→	SwimLaneID     *int64   `json:"swim_lane_id,omitempty"`
    55→	DueDate        *string  `json:"due_date,omitempty"`
    56→	SprintID       *int64   `json:"sprint_id,omitempty"`
    57→	Priority       *string  `json:"priority,omitempty"`
    58→	AssigneeID     *int64   `json:"assignee_id,omitempty"`
    59→	EstimatedHours *float64 `json:"estimated_hours,omitempty"`
    60→	ActualHours    *float64 `json:"actual_hours,omitempty"`
    61→	TagIDs         *[]int64 `json:"tag_ids,omitempty"`
    62→}
    63→
    64→// HandleListTasks returns all tasks for a project
    65→func (s *Server) HandleListTasks(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    66→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
    67→	defer cancel()
    68→
    69→	userID := r.Context().Value(UserIDKey).(int64)
    70→	projectID, err := strconv.ParseInt(chi.URLParam(r, "projectId"), 10, 64)
    71→	if err != nil {
    72→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid project ID", "invalid_input")
    73→		return
    74→	}
    75→
    76→	// Verify user has access to this project
    77→	hasAccess, err := s.checkProjectAccess(ctx, userID, projectID)
    78→	if err != nil {
    79→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to verify project access", "internal_error")
    80→		return
    81→	}
    82→	if !hasAccess {
    83→		respondError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "access denied", "forbidden")
    84→		return
    85→	}
    86→
    87→	// First, fetch all tasks with assignee, sprint, and swim lane names
    88→	query := `
    89→		SELECT t.id, t.project_id, t.task_number, t.title, t.description, t.status, t.swim_lane_id, sl.name as swim_lane_name, t.due_date,
    90→		       t.sprint_id, s.name as sprint_name,
    91→		       t.priority, t.assignee_id, u.name as assignee_name,
    92→		       t.estimated_hours, t.actual_hours, t.created_at, t.updated_at
    93→		FROM tasks t
    94→		LEFT JOIN users u ON t.assignee_id = u.id
    95→		LEFT JOIN sprints s ON t.sprint_id = s.id
    96→		LEFT JOIN swim_lanes sl ON t.swim_lane_id = sl.id
    97→		WHERE t.project_id = ?
    98→		ORDER BY t.created_at DESC
    99→	`
   100→
   101→	rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, query, projectID)
   102→	if err != nil {
   103→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to fetch tasks", "internal_error")
   104→		return
   105→	}
   106→	defer rows.Close()
   107→
   108→	tasks := []Task{}
   109→	taskIDs := []int64{}
   110→	taskMap := make(map[int64]*Task)
   111→
   112→	for rows.Next() {
   113→		var t Task
   114→		var priority sql.NullString
   115→		var taskNumber sql.NullInt64
   116→		if err := rows.Scan(&t.ID, &t.ProjectID, &taskNumber, &t.Title, &t.Description, &t.Status, &t.SwimLaneID, &t.SwimLaneName, &t.DueDate,
   117→			&t.SprintID, &t.SprintName, &priority, &t.AssigneeID, &t.AssigneeName,
   118→			&t.EstimatedHours, &t.ActualHours, &t.CreatedAt, &t.UpdatedAt); err != nil {
   119→			respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to scan task", "internal_error")
   120→			return
   121→		}
   122→		t.Priority = "medium" // default
   123→		if priority.Valid {
   124→			t.Priority = priority.String
   125→		}
   126→		if taskNumber.Valid {
   127→			t.TaskNumber = taskNumber.Int64
   128→		}
   129→		t.Tags = []Tag{} // Initialize empty tags array
   130→
   131→		tasks = append(tasks, t)
   132→		taskIDs = append(taskIDs, t.ID)
   133→		taskMap[t.ID] = &tasks[len(tasks)-1]
   134→	}
   135→
   136→	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
   137→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "error iterating tasks", "internal_error")
   138→		return
   139→	}
   140→
   141→	// If there are tasks, fetch all tags in a single query
   142→	if len(taskIDs) > 0 {
   143→		// Build IN clause for task IDs
   144→		placeholders := ""
   145→		args := make([]interface{}, len(taskIDs))
   146→		for i, id := range taskIDs {
   147→			if i > 0 {
   148→				placeholders += ","
   149→			}
   150→			placeholders += "?"
   151→			args[i] = id
   152→		}
   153→
   154→		tagQuery := `
   155→			SELECT tt.task_id, t.id, t.user_id, t.name, t.color, t.created_at
   156→			FROM tags t
   157→			JOIN task_tags tt ON t.id = tt.tag_id
   158→			WHERE tt.task_id IN (` + placeholders + `)
   159→			ORDER BY tt.task_id, t.name
   160→		`
   161→
   162→		tagRows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, tagQuery, args...)
   163→		if err == nil {
   164→			defer tagRows.Close()
   165→			for tagRows.Next() {
   166→				var taskID int64
   167→				var tag Tag
   168→				if err := tagRows.Scan(&taskID, &tag.ID, &tag.UserID, &tag.Name, &tag.Color, &tag.CreatedAt); err == nil {
   169→					if task, exists := taskMap[taskID]; exists {
   170→						task.Tags = append(task.Tags, tag)
   171→					}
   172→				}
   173→			}
   174→		}
   175→	}
   176→
   177→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, tasks)
   178→}
   179→
   180→// HandleCreateTask creates a new task
   181→func (s *Server) HandleCreateTask(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   182→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
   183→	defer cancel()
   184→
   185→	userID := r.Context().Value(UserIDKey).(int64)
   186→	projectID, err := strconv.ParseInt(chi.URLParam(r, "projectId"), 10, 64)
   187→	if err != nil {
   188→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid project ID", "invalid_input")
   189→		return
   190→	}
   191→
   192→	// Verify user has access to this project
   193→	hasAccess, err := s.checkProjectAccess(ctx, userID, projectID)
   194→	if err != nil {
   195→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to verify project access", "internal_error")
   196→		return
   197→	}
   198→	if !hasAccess {
   199→		respondError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "access denied", "forbidden")
   200→		return
   201→	}
   202→
   203→	var req CreateTaskRequest
   204→	if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {
   205→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid request body", "invalid_input")
   206→		return
   207→	}
   208→
   209→	// Validation
   210→	if req.Title == "" {
   211→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "task title is required", "invalid_input")
   212→		return
   213→	}
   214→	if len(req.Title) > 255 {
   215→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "task title is too long (max 255 characters)", "invalid_input")
   216→		return
   217→	}
   218→
   219→	// Default status to 'todo' if not provided (for backward compatibility)
   220→	status := "todo"
   221→	if req.Status != nil {
   222→		status = *req.Status
   223→	}
   224→
   225→	// Validate status
   226→	if status != "todo" && status != "in_progress" && status != "done" {
   227→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid status (must be: todo, in_progress, or done)", "invalid_input")
   228→		return
   229→	}
   230→
   231→	// Sync swim_lane_id and status
   232→	var swimLaneID *int64
   233→	if req.SwimLaneID != nil {
   234→		swimLaneID = req.SwimLaneID
   235→		// Derive status from the swim lane's status_category
   236→		var category string
   237→		laneQuery := `SELECT status_category FROM swim_lanes WHERE id = ? AND project_id = ?`
   238→		if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, laneQuery, *req.SwimLaneID, projectID).Scan(&category); err == nil && category != "" {
   239→			status = category
   240→		}
   241→	} else {
   242→		// Find first swim lane matching the status category
   243→		var laneID int64
   244→		laneQuery := `SELECT id FROM swim_lanes WHERE project_id = ? AND status_category = ? ORDER BY position ASC LIMIT 1`
   245→		if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, laneQuery, projectID, status).Scan(&laneID); err == nil {
   246→			swimLaneID = &laneID
   247→		}
   248→	}
   249→
   250→	// Default priority
   251→	priority := "medium"
   252→	if req.Priority != nil {
   253→		priority = *req.Priority
   254→	}
   255→
   256→	// Validate priority
   257→	if priority != "low" && priority != "medium" && priority != "high" && priority != "urgent" {
   258→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid priority (must be: low, medium, high, or urgent)", "invalid_input")
   259→		return
   260→	}
   261→
   262→	// Assign task_number atomically using a transaction
   263→	tx, err := s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
   264→	if err != nil {
   265→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to start transaction", "internal_error")
   266→		return
   267→	}
   268→	defer tx.Rollback()
   269→
   270→	// Get next task_number for this project
   271→	var nextNumber int64
   272→	err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT COALESCE(MAX(task_number), 0) + 1 FROM tasks WHERE project_id = ?`, projectID).Scan(&nextNumber)
   273→	if err != nil {
   274→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to get next task number", "internal_error")
   275→		return
   276→	}
   277→
   278→	query := `
   279→		INSERT INTO tasks (project_id, task_number, title, description, status, swim_lane_id, due_date, sprint_id, priority, assignee_id, estimated_hours, actual_hours, created_at, updated_at)
   280→		VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
   281→	`
   282→
   283→	result, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, query, projectID, nextNumber, req.Title, req.Description, status, swimLaneID, req.DueDate, req.SprintID, priority, req.AssigneeID, req.EstimatedHours, req.ActualHours)
   284→	if err != nil {
   285→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to create task", "internal_error")
   286→		return
   287→	}
   288→
   289→	taskID, err := result.LastInsertId()
   290→	if err != nil {
   291→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to get task ID", "internal_error")
   292→		return
   293→	}
   294→
   295→	// Add tags if provided
   296→	if len(req.TagIDs) > 0 {
   297→		for _, tagID := range req.TagIDs {
   298→			_, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, "INSERT INTO task_tags (task_id, tag_id) VALUES (?, ?)", taskID, tagID)
   299→			if err != nil {
   300→				// Continue even if tag insertion fails
   301→				continue
   302→			}
   303→		}
   304→	}
   305→
   306→	if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
   307→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to commit task creation", "internal_error")
   308→		return
   309→	}
   310→
   311→	// Fetch the created task with assignee, sprint, and swim lane names
   312→	var t Task
   313→	var priorityVal sql.NullString
   314→	fetchQuery := `
   315→		SELECT t.id, t.project_id, t.task_number, t.title, t.description, t.status, t.swim_lane_id, sl.name as swim_lane_name, t.due_date,
   316→		       t.sprint_id, s.name as sprint_name,
   317→		       t.priority, t.assignee_id, u.name as assignee_name,
   318→		       t.estimated_hours, t.actual_hours, t.created_at, t.updated_at
   319→		FROM tasks t
   320→		LEFT JOIN users u ON t.assignee_id = u.id
   321→		LEFT JOIN sprints s ON t.sprint_id = s.id
   322→		LEFT JOIN swim_lanes sl ON t.swim_lane_id = sl.id
   323→		WHERE t.id = ?
   324→	`
   325→	err = s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, fetchQuery, taskID).Scan(
   326→		&t.ID, &t.ProjectID, &t.TaskNumber, &t.Title, &t.Description, &t.Status, &t.SwimLaneID, &t.SwimLaneName, &t.DueDate,
   327→		&t.SprintID, &t.SprintName, &priorityVal, &t.AssigneeID, &t.AssigneeName,
   328→		&t.EstimatedHours, &t.ActualHours, &t.CreatedAt, &t.UpdatedAt,
   329→	)
   330→	if err != nil {
   331→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to fetch created task", "internal_error")
   332→		return
   333→	}
   334→
   335→	t.Priority = "medium" // default
   336→	if priorityVal.Valid {
   337→		t.Priority = priorityVal.String
   338→	}
   339→
   340→	// Fetch tags
   341→	tagQuery := `
   342→		SELECT t.id, t.user_id, t.name, t.color, t.created_at
   343→		FROM tags t
   344→		JOIN task_tags tt ON t.id = tt.tag_id
   345→		WHERE tt.task_id = ?
   346→	`
   347→	tagRows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, tagQuery, taskID)
   348→	if err == nil {
   349→		defer tagRows.Close()
   350→		t.Tags = []Tag{}
   351→		for tagRows.Next() {
   352→			var tag Tag
   353→			if err := tagRows.Scan(&tag.ID, &tag.UserID, &tag.Name, &tag.Color, &tag.CreatedAt); err == nil {
   354→				t.Tags = append(t.Tags, tag)
   355→			}
   356→		}
   357→	}
   358→
   359→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, t)
   360→}
   361→
   362→// HandleUpdateTask updates an existing task
   363→func (s *Server) HandleUpdateTask(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   364→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
   365→	defer cancel()
   366→
   367→	userID := r.Context().Value(UserIDKey).(int64)
   368→	taskID, err := strconv.ParseInt(chi.URLParam(r, "id"), 10, 64)
   369→	if err != nil {
   370→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid task ID", "invalid_input")
   371→		return
   372→	}
   373→
   374→	var req UpdateTaskRequest
   375→	if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {
   376→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid request body", "invalid_input")
   377→		return
   378→	}
   379→
   380→	// Get task's project ID and verify user has access
   381→	var projectID int64
   382→	projectQuery := `SELECT project_id FROM tasks WHERE id = ?`
   383→	if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, projectQuery, taskID).Scan(&projectID); err == sql.ErrNoRows {
   384→		respondError(w, http.StatusNotFound, "task not found", "not_found")
   385→		return
   386→	} else if err != nil {
   387→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to get task project", "internal_error")
   388→		return
   389→	}
   390→
   391→	// Verify user has access to the project
   392→	hasAccess, err := s.checkProjectAccess(ctx, userID, projectID)
   393→	if err != nil {
   394→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to verify project access", "internal_error")
   395→		return
   396→	}
   397→	if !hasAccess {
   398→		respondError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "access denied", "forbidden")
   399→		return
   400→	}
   401→
   402→	// Build update query dynamically
   403→	query := "UPDATE tasks SET updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP"
   404→	args := []interface{}{}
   405→
   406→	if req.Title != nil {
   407→		if *req.Title == "" {
   408→			respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "task title cannot be empty", "invalid_input")
   409→			return
   410→		}
   411→		if len(*req.Title) > 255 {
   412→			respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "task title is too long (max 255 characters)", "invalid_input")
   413→			return
   414→		}
   415→		query += ", title = ?"
   416→		args = append(args, *req.Title)
   417→	}
   418→
   419→	if req.Description != nil {
   420→		query += ", description = ?"
   421→		args = append(args, *req.Description)
   422→	}
   423→
   424→	// Swim lane/status sync: if swim_lane_id changes, derive status; if status changes, find matching lane
   425→	if req.SwimLaneID != nil && req.Status == nil {
   426→		// Swim lane changed — derive status from lane's status_category
   427→		var category string
   428→		laneQuery := `SELECT status_category FROM swim_lanes WHERE id = ? AND project_id = ?`
   429→		if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, laneQuery, *req.SwimLaneID, projectID).Scan(&category); err == nil && category != "" {
   430→			query += ", status = ?"
   431→			args = append(args, category)
   432→		}
   433→		query += ", swim_lane_id = ?"
   434→		args = append(args, *req.SwimLaneID)
   435→	} else if req.Status != nil && req.SwimLaneID == nil {
   436→		// Status changed — find first swim lane with matching status_category
   437→		if *req.Status != "todo" && *req.Status != "in_progress" && *req.Status != "done" {
   438→			respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid status (must be: todo, in_progress, or done)", "invalid_input")
   439→			return
   440→		}
   441→		query += ", status = ?"
   442→		args = append(args, *req.Status)
   443→		var laneID int64
   444→		laneQuery := `SELECT id FROM swim_lanes WHERE project_id = ? AND status_category = ? ORDER BY position ASC LIMIT 1`
   445→		if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, laneQuery, projectID, *req.Status).Scan(&laneID); err == nil {
   446→			query += ", swim_lane_id = ?"
   447→			args = append(args, laneID)
   448→		}
   449→	} else if req.Status != nil && req.SwimLaneID != nil {
   450→		// Both provided — trust swim_lane_id, derive status from it
   451→		if *req.Status != "todo" && *req.Status != "in_progress" && *req.Status != "done" {
   452→			respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid status (must be: todo, in_progress, or done)", "invalid_input")
   453→			return
   454→		}
   455→		var category string
   456→		laneQuery := `SELECT status_category FROM swim_lanes WHERE id = ? AND project_id = ?`
   457→		if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, laneQuery, *req.SwimLaneID, projectID).Scan(&category); err == nil && category != "" {
   458→			query += ", status = ?"
   459→			args = append(args, category)
   460→		} else {
   461→			query += ", status = ?"
   462→			args = append(args, *req.Status)
   463→		}
   464→		query += ", swim_lane_id = ?"
   465→		args = append(args, *req.SwimLaneID)
   466→	}
   467→
   468→	if req.DueDate != nil {
   469→		query += ", due_date = ?"
   470→		args = append(args, *req.DueDate)
   471→	}
   472→
   473→	if req.Priority != nil {
   474→		if *req.Priority != "low" && *req.Priority != "medium" && *req.Priority != "high" && *req.Priority != "urgent" {
   475→			respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid priority (must be: low, medium, high, or urgent)", "invalid_input")
   476→			return
   477→		}
   478→		query += ", priority = ?"
   479→		args = append(args, *req.Priority)
   480→	}
   481→
   482→	if req.SprintID != nil {
   483→		query += ", sprint_id = ?"
   484→		args = append(args, *req.SprintID)
   485→	}
   486→
   487→	if req.AssigneeID != nil {
   488→		query += ", assignee_id = ?"
   489→		args = append(args, *req.AssigneeID)
   490→	}
   491→
   492→	if req.EstimatedHours != nil {
   493→		query += ", estimated_hours = ?"
   494→		args = append(args, *req.EstimatedHours)
   495→	}
   496→
   497→	if req.ActualHours != nil {
   498→		query += ", actual_hours = ?"
   499→		args = append(args, *req.ActualHours)
   500→	}
   501→
   502→	query += " WHERE id = ?"
   503→	args = append(args, taskID)
   504→
   505→	_, err = s.db.ExecContext(ctx, query, args...)
   506→	if err != nil {
   507→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to update task", "internal_error")
   508→		return
   509→	}
   510→
   511→	// Fetch the updated task with assignee, sprint, and swim lane names
   512→	var t Task
   513→	var priorityVal sql.NullString
   514→	fetchQuery := `
   515→		SELECT t.id, t.project_id, t.task_number, t.title, t.description, t.status, t.swim_lane_id, sl.name as swim_lane_name, t.due_date,
   516→		       t.sprint_id, s.name as sprint_name,
   517→		       t.priority, t.assignee_id, u.name as assignee_name,
   518→		       t.estimated_hours, t.actual_hours, t.created_at, t.updated_at
   519→		FROM tasks t
   520→		LEFT JOIN users u ON t.assignee_id = u.id
   521→		LEFT JOIN sprints s ON t.sprint_id = s.id
   522→		LEFT JOIN swim_lanes sl ON t.swim_lane_id = sl.id
   523→		WHERE t.id = ?
   524→	`
   525→	err = s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, fetchQuery, taskID).Scan(
   526→		&t.ID, &t.ProjectID, &t.TaskNumber, &t.Title, &t.Description, &t.Status, &t.SwimLaneID, &t.SwimLaneName, &t.DueDate,
   527→		&t.SprintID, &t.SprintName, &priorityVal, &t.AssigneeID, &t.AssigneeName,
   528→		&t.EstimatedHours, &t.ActualHours, &t.CreatedAt, &t.UpdatedAt,
   529→	)
   530→	if err != nil {
   531→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to fetch updated task", "internal_error")
   532→		return
   533→	}
   534→
   535→	t.Priority = "medium" // default
   536→	if priorityVal.Valid {
   537→		t.Priority = priorityVal.String
   538→	}
   539→
   540→	// Fetch tags
   541→	tagQuery := `
   542→		SELECT t.id, t.user_id, t.name, t.color, t.created_at
   543→		FROM tags t
   544→		JOIN task_tags tt ON t.id = tt.tag_id
   545→		WHERE tt.task_id = ?
   546→	`
   547→	tagRows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, tagQuery, taskID)
   548→	if err == nil {
   549→		defer tagRows.Close()
   550→		t.Tags = []Tag{}
   551→		for tagRows.Next() {
   552→			var tag Tag
   553→			if err := tagRows.Scan(&tag.ID, &tag.UserID, &tag.Name, &tag.Color, &tag.CreatedAt); err == nil {
   554→				t.Tags = append(t.Tags, tag)
   555→			}
   556→		}
   557→	}
   558→
   559→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, t)
   560→}
   561→
   562→// HandleDeleteTask deletes a task
   563→func (s *Server) HandleDeleteTask(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   564→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
   565→	defer cancel()
   566→
   567→	userID := r.Context().Value(UserIDKey).(int64)
   568→	taskID, err := strconv.ParseInt(chi.URLParam(r, "id"), 10, 64)
   569→	if err != nil {
   570→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid task ID", "invalid_input")
   571→		return
   572→	}
   573→
   574→	// Get task's project ID and verify user has access
   575→	var projectID int64
   576→	projectQuery := `SELECT project_id FROM tasks WHERE id = ?`
   577→	if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, projectQuery, taskID).Scan(&projectID); err == sql.ErrNoRows {
   578→		respondError(w, http.StatusNotFound, "task not found", "not_found")
   579→		return
   580→	} else if err != nil {
   581→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to get task project", "internal_error")
   582→		return
   583→	}
   584→
   585→	// Verify user has access to the project
   586→	hasAccess, err := s.checkProjectAccess(ctx, userID, projectID)
   587→	if err != nil {
   588→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to verify project access", "internal_error")
   589→		return
   590→	}
   591→	if !hasAccess {
   592→		respondError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "access denied", "forbidden")
   593→		return
   594→	}
   595→
   596→	query := `DELETE FROM tasks WHERE id = ?`
   597→	result, err := s.db.ExecContext(ctx, query, taskID)
   598→	if err != nil {
   599→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to delete task", "internal_error")
   600→		return
   601→	}
   602→
   603→	rowsAffected, err := result.RowsAffected()
   604→	if err != nil {
   605→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to verify deletion", "internal_error")
   606→		return
   607→	}
   608→
   609→	if rowsAffected == 0 {
   610→		respondError(w, http.StatusNotFound, "task not found", "not_found")
   611→		return
   612→	}
   613→
   614→	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
   615→}
   616→
   617→// HandleGetTaskByNumber returns a single task by project-scoped task number
   618→func (s *Server) HandleGetTaskByNumber(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   619→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
   620→	defer cancel()
   621→
   622→	userID := r.Context().Value(UserIDKey).(int64)
   623→	projectID, err := strconv.ParseInt(chi.URLParam(r, "projectId"), 10, 64)
   624→	if err != nil {
   625→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid project ID", "invalid_input")
   626→		return
   627→	}
   628→	taskNumber, err := strconv.ParseInt(chi.URLParam(r, "taskNumber"), 10, 64)
   629→	if err != nil {
   630→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid task number", "invalid_input")
   631→		return
   632→	}
   633→
   634→	// Verify user has access to this project
   635→	hasAccess, err := s.checkProjectAccess(ctx, userID, projectID)
   636→	if err != nil {
   637→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to verify project access", "internal_error")
   638→		return
   639→	}
   640→	if !hasAccess {
   641→		respondError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "access denied", "forbidden")
   642→		return
   643→	}
   644→
   645→	var t Task
   646→	var priorityVal sql.NullString
   647→	fetchQuery := `
   648→		SELECT t.id, t.project_id, t.task_number, t.title, t.description, t.status, t.swim_lane_id, sl.name as swim_lane_name, t.due_date,
   649→		       t.sprint_id, s.name as sprint_name,
   650→		       t.priority, t.assignee_id, u.name as assignee_name,
   651→		       t.estimated_hours, t.actual_hours, t.created_at, t.updated_at
   652→		FROM tasks t
   653→		LEFT JOIN users u ON t.assignee_id = u.id
   654→		LEFT JOIN sprints s ON t.sprint_id = s.id
   655→		LEFT JOIN swim_lanes sl ON t.swim_lane_id = sl.id
   656→		WHERE t.project_id = ? AND t.task_number = ?
   657→	`
   658→	err = s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, fetchQuery, projectID, taskNumber).Scan(
   659→		&t.ID, &t.ProjectID, &t.TaskNumber, &t.Title, &t.Description, &t.Status, &t.SwimLaneID, &t.SwimLaneName, &t.DueDate,
   660→		&t.SprintID, &t.SprintName, &priorityVal, &t.AssigneeID, &t.AssigneeName,
   661→		&t.EstimatedHours, &t.ActualHours, &t.CreatedAt, &t.UpdatedAt,
   662→	)
   663→	if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
   664→		respondError(w, http.StatusNotFound, "task not found", "not_found")
   665→		return
   666→	}
   667→	if err != nil {
   668→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to fetch task", "internal_error")
   669→		return
   670→	}
   671→
   672→	t.Priority = "medium"
   673→	if priorityVal.Valid {
   674→		t.Priority = priorityVal.String
   675→	}
   676→
   677→	// Fetch tags
   678→	tagQuery := `
   679→		SELECT tg.id, tg.user_id, tg.name, tg.color, tg.created_at
   680→		FROM tags tg
   681→		JOIN task_tags tt ON tg.id = tt.tag_id
   682→		WHERE tt.task_id = ?
   683→	`
   684→	tagRows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, tagQuery, t.ID)
   685→	if err == nil {
   686→		defer tagRows.Close()
   687→		t.Tags = []Tag{}
   688→		for tagRows.Next() {
   689→			var tag Tag
   690→			if err := tagRows.Scan(&tag.ID, &tag.UserID, &tag.Name, &tag.Color, &tag.CreatedAt); err == nil {
   691→				t.Tags = append(t.Tags, tag)
   692→			}
   693→		}
   694→	}
   695→
   696→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, t)
   697→}
   698→
   699→// checkProjectAccess verifies that a user has access to a project via project_members table
   700→func (s *Server) checkProjectAccess(ctx context.Context, userID, projectID int64) (bool, error) {
   701→	var hasAccess bool
   702→	query := `
   703→		SELECT EXISTS(
   704→			SELECT 1 FROM project_members
   705→			WHERE project_id = ? AND user_id = ?
   706→		)
   707→	`
   708→	err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, query, projectID, userID).Scan(&hasAccess)
   709→	return hasAccess, err
   710→}
   711→

data.prompt:
Explore the SprintSpark codebase thoroughly to understand the swim lane / status synchronization logic. The issue is that tasks can end up with mismatched status and swim lane (e.g., status="done" but swim_lane_id pointing to "To Do" column, or vice versa).

Look at:
1. Backend API handlers for task updates (PATCH /api/tasks/:id) - how does it handle swim_lane_id and status fields when one is updated?
2. Backend API handlers for task creation - does it sync status with swim_lane?
3. Frontend code that updates tasks (drag and drop on kanban board, status dropdown changes, swim lane changes)
4. The database schema for tasks and swim_lanes tables
5. The recent commit "feat: fix swim lane/status sync" to see what was already fixed

Focus on finding any code paths where status and swim_lane_id can get out of sync. Be very thorough - check all update paths including drag-and-drop, inline editing, task creation, and API endpoints.

3. Frontend code that updates tasks (drag and drop on kanban board, status dropdown changes, swim lane changes)
4. The database schema for tasks and swim_lanes tables
5. The recent commit "feat: fix swim lane/status sync" to see what was already fixed

Focus on finding any code paths where status and swim_lane_id can get out of sync. Be very thorough - check all update paths including drag-and-drop, inline editing, task creation, and API endpoints.

content.content:
1→package api
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"database/sql"
     6→	"encoding/json"
     7→	"net/http"
     8→	"strconv"
     9→	"time"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/go-chi/chi/v5"
    12→	"go.uber.org/zap"
    13→)
    14→
    15→type SwimLane struct {
    16→	ID             int64     `json:"id"`
    17→	ProjectID      int64     `json:"project_id"`
    18→	Name           string    `json:"name"`
    19→	Color          string    `json:"color"`
    20→	Position       int       `json:"position"`
    21→	StatusCategory string    `json:"status_category"`
    22→	CreatedAt      time.Time `json:"created_at"`
    23→	UpdatedAt      time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
    24→}
    25→
    26→type CreateSwimLaneRequest struct {
    27→	Name           string `json:"name"`
    28→	Color          string `json:"color"`
    29→	Position       int    `json:"position"`
    30→	StatusCategory string `json:"status_category"`
    31→}
    32→
    33→type UpdateSwimLaneRequest struct {
    34→	Name           *string `json:"name,omitempty"`
    35→	Color          *string `json:"color,omitempty"`
    36→	Position       *int    `json:"position,omitempty"`
    37→	StatusCategory *string `json:"status_category,omitempty"`
    38→}
    39→
    40→// HandleListSwimLanes returns all swim lanes for a project
    41→func (s *Server) HandleListSwimLanes(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    42→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
    43→	defer cancel()
    44→
    45→	userID := r.Context().Value(UserIDKey).(int64)
    46→	projectID, err := strconv.ParseInt(chi.URLParam(r, "projectId"), 10, 64)
    47→	if err != nil {
    48→		s.logger.Warn("Invalid project ID", zap.Error(err))
    49→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid project ID", "invalid_input")
    50→		return
    51→	}
    52→
    53→	// Verify user has access to this project
    54→	hasAccess, err := s.checkProjectAccess(ctx, userID, projectID)
    55→	if err != nil {
    56→		s.logger.Error("Failed to verify project access", zap.Error(err), zap.Int64("userID", userID), zap.Int64("projectID", projectID))
    57→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to verify project access", "internal_error")
    58→		return
    59→	}
    60→	if !hasAccess {
    61→		s.logger.Warn("Access denied to project", zap.Int64("userID", userID), zap.Int64("projectID", projectID))
    62→		respondError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "access denied", "forbidden")
    63→		return
    64→	}
    65→
    66→	query := `
    67→		SELECT id, project_id, name, color, position, status_category, created_at, updated_at
    68→		FROM swim_lanes
    69→		WHERE project_id = ?
    70→		ORDER BY position ASC
    71→	`
    72→
    73→	rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, query, projectID)
    74→	if err != nil {
    75→		s.logger.Error("Failed to fetch swim lanes", zap.Error(err), zap.Int64("projectID", projectID))
    76→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to fetch swim lanes", "internal_error")
    77→		return
    78→	}
    79→	defer rows.Close()
    80→
    81→	swimLanes := []SwimLane{}
    82→	for rows.Next() {
    83→		var sl SwimLane
    84→		if err := rows.Scan(&sl.ID, &sl.ProjectID, &sl.Name, &sl.Color, &sl.Position, &sl.StatusCategory, &sl.CreatedAt, &sl.UpdatedAt); err != nil {
    85→			s.logger.Error("Failed to scan swim lane", zap.Error(err))
    86→			respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to scan swim lane", "internal_error")
    87→			return
    88→		}
    89→		swimLanes = append(swimLanes, sl)
    90→	}
    91→
    92→	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
    93→		s.logger.Error("Error iterating swim lanes", zap.Error(err))
    94→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "error iterating swim lanes", "internal_error")
    95→		return
    96→	}
    97→
    98→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, swimLanes)
    99→}
   100→
   101→// HandleCreateSwimLane creates a new swim lane
   102→func (s *Server) HandleCreateSwimLane(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   103→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
   104→	defer cancel()
   105→
   106→	userID := r.Context().Value(UserIDKey).(int64)
   107→	projectID, err := strconv.ParseInt(chi.URLParam(r, "projectId"), 10, 64)
   108→	if err != nil {
   109→		s.logger.Warn("Invalid project ID", zap.Error(err))
   110→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid project ID", "invalid_input")
   111→		return
   112→	}
   113→
   114→	// Verify user has access to this project
   115→	hasAccess, err := s.checkProjectAccess(ctx, userID, projectID)
   116→	if err != nil {
   117→		s.logger.Error("Failed to verify project access", zap.Error(err), zap.Int64("userID", userID), zap.Int64("projectID", projectID))
   118→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to verify project access", "internal_error")
   119→		return
   120→	}
   121→	if !hasAccess {
   122→		s.logger.Warn("Access denied to project", zap.Int64("userID", userID), zap.Int64("projectID", projectID))
   123→		respondError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "access denied", "forbidden")
   124→		return
   125→	}
   126→
   127→	var req CreateSwimLaneRequest
   128→	if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {
   129→		s.logger.Warn("Invalid request body", zap.Error(err))
   130→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid request body", "invalid_input")
   131→		return
   132→	}
   133→
   134→	// Validation
   135→	if req.Name == "" {
   136→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "swim lane name is required", "invalid_input")
   137→		return
   138→	}
   139→	if len(req.Name) > 50 {
   140→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "swim lane name is too long (max 50 characters)", "invalid_input")
   141→		return
   142→	}
   143→	if req.Color == "" {
   144→		req.Color = "#6B7280" // default gray
   145→	}
   146→
   147→	// Validate status_category
   148→	if req.StatusCategory == "" {
   149→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "status_category is required (must be: todo, in_progress, or done)", "invalid_input")
   150→		return
   151→	}
   152→	if req.StatusCategory != "todo" && req.StatusCategory != "in_progress" && req.StatusCategory != "done" {
   153→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid status_category (must be: todo, in_progress, or done)", "invalid_input")
   154→		return
   155→	}
   156→
   157→	// Check swim lane count limit (max 6)
   158→	var count int
   159→	countQuery := `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM swim_lanes WHERE project_id = ?`
   160→	if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, countQuery, projectID).Scan(&count); err != nil {
   161→		s.logger.Error("Failed to count swim lanes", zap.Error(err), zap.Int64("projectID", projectID))
   162→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to count swim lanes", "internal_error")
   163→		return
   164→	}
   165→	if count >= 6 {
   166→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "maximum 6 swim lanes allowed per project", "max_limit_reached")
   167→		return
   168→	}
   169→
   170→	// Check minimum (need at least 2)
   171→	if req.Position < 0 {
   172→		req.Position = 0
   173→	}
   174→
   175→	query := `
   176→		INSERT INTO swim_lanes (project_id, name, color, position, status_category, created_at, updated_at)
   177→		VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
   178→	`
   179→
   180→	result, err := s.db.ExecContext(ctx, query, projectID, req.Name, req.Color, req.Position, req.StatusCategory)
   181→	if err != nil {
   182→		s.logger.Error("Failed to create swim lane", zap.Error(err), zap.Int64("projectID", projectID), zap.String("name", req.Name))
   183→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to create swim lane", "internal_error")
   184→		return
   185→	}
   186→
   187→	swimLaneID, err := result.LastInsertId()
   188→	if err != nil {
   189→		s.logger.Error("Failed to get swim lane ID", zap.Error(err))
   190→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to get swim lane ID", "internal_error")
   191→		return
   192→	}
   193→
   194→	// Fetch the created swim lane
   195→	var sl SwimLane
   196→	fetchQuery := `
   197→		SELECT id, project_id, name, color, position, status_category, created_at, updated_at
   198→		FROM swim_lanes
   199→		WHERE id = ?
   200→	`
   201→	err = s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, fetchQuery, swimLaneID).Scan(
   202→		&sl.ID, &sl.ProjectID, &sl.Name, &sl.Color, &sl.Position, &sl.StatusCategory, &sl.CreatedAt, &sl.UpdatedAt,
   203→	)
   204→	if err != nil {
   205→		s.logger.Error("Failed to fetch created swim lane", zap.Error(err), zap.Int64("swimLaneID", swimLaneID))
   206→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to fetch created swim lane", "internal_error")
   207→		return
   208→	}
   209→
   210→	s.logger.Info("Swim lane created", zap.Int64("swimLaneID", swimLaneID), zap.Int64("projectID", projectID), zap.String("name", req.Name))
   211→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, sl)
   212→}
   213→
   214→// HandleUpdateSwimLane updates an existing swim lane
   215→func (s *Server) HandleUpdateSwimLane(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   216→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
   217→	defer cancel()
   218→
   219→	userID := r.Context().Value(UserIDKey).(int64)
   220→	swimLaneID, err := strconv.ParseInt(chi.URLParam(r, "id"), 10, 64)
   221→	if err != nil {
   222→		s.logger.Warn("Invalid swim lane ID", zap.Error(err))
   223→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid swim lane ID", "invalid_input")
   224→		return
   225→	}
   226→
   227→	var req UpdateSwimLaneRequest
   228→	if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {
   229→		s.logger.Warn("Invalid request body", zap.Error(err))
   230→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid request body", "invalid_input")
   231→		return
   232→	}
   233→
   234→	// Get swim lane's project ID and verify user has access
   235→	var projectID int64
   236→	projectQuery := `SELECT project_id FROM swim_lanes WHERE id = ?`
   237→	if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, projectQuery, swimLaneID).Scan(&projectID); err == sql.ErrNoRows {
   238→		respondError(w, http.StatusNotFound, "swim lane not found", "not_found")
   239→		return
   240→	} else if err != nil {
   241→		s.logger.Error("Failed to get swim lane project", zap.Error(err), zap.Int64("swimLaneID", swimLaneID))
   242→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to get swim lane project", "internal_error")
   243→		return
   244→	}
   245→
   246→	// Verify user has access to the project
   247→	hasAccess, err := s.checkProjectAccess(ctx, userID, projectID)
   248→	if err != nil {
   249→		s.logger.Error("Failed to verify project access", zap.Error(err), zap.Int64("userID", userID), zap.Int64("projectID", projectID))
   250→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to verify project access", "internal_error")
   251→		return
   252→	}
   253→	if !hasAccess {
   254→		s.logger.Warn("Access denied to project", zap.Int64("userID", userID), zap.Int64("projectID", projectID))
   255→		respondError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "access denied", "forbidden")
   256→		return
   257→	}
   258→
   259→	// Build update query dynamically
   260→	query := "UPDATE swim_lanes SET updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP"
   261→	args := []interface{}{}
   262→
   263→	if req.Name != nil {
   264→		if *req.Name == "" {
   265→			respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "swim lane name cannot be empty", "invalid_input")
   266→			return
   267→		}
   268→		if len(*req.Name) > 50 {
   269→			respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "swim lane name is too long (max 50 characters)", "invalid_input")
   270→			return
   271→		}
   272→		query += ", name = ?"
   273→		args = append(args, *req.Name)
   274→	}
   275→
   276→	if req.Color != nil {
   277→		query += ", color = ?"
   278→		args = append(args, *req.Color)
   279→	}
   280→
   281→	if req.Position != nil {
   282→		if *req.Position < 0 {
   283→			respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "position cannot be negative", "invalid_input")
   284→			return
   285→		}
   286→		query += ", position = ?"
   287→		args = append(args, *req.Position)
   288→	}
   289→
   290→	if req.StatusCategory != nil {
   291→		if *req.StatusCategory != "todo" && *req.StatusCategory != "in_progress" && *req.StatusCategory != "done" {
   292→			respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid status_category (must be: todo, in_progress, or done)", "invalid_input")
   293→			return
   294→		}
   295→		query += ", status_category = ?"
   296→		args = append(args, *req.StatusCategory)
   297→	}
   298→
   299→	query += " WHERE id = ?"
   300→	args = append(args, swimLaneID)
   301→
   302→	_, err = s.db.ExecContext(ctx, query, args...)
   303→	if err != nil {
   304→		s.logger.Error("Failed to update swim lane", zap.Error(err), zap.Int64("swimLaneID", swimLaneID))
   305→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to update swim lane", "internal_error")
   306→		return
   307→	}
   308→
   309→	// Fetch the updated swim lane
   310→	var sl SwimLane
   311→	fetchQuery := `
   312→		SELECT id, project_id, name, color, position, status_category, created_at, updated_at
   313→		FROM swim_lanes
   314→		WHERE id = ?
   315→	`
   316→	err = s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, fetchQuery, swimLaneID).Scan(
   317→		&sl.ID, &sl.ProjectID, &sl.Name, &sl.Color, &sl.Position, &sl.StatusCategory, &sl.CreatedAt, &sl.UpdatedAt,
   318→	)
   319→	if err != nil {
   320→		s.logger.Error("Failed to fetch updated swim lane", zap.Error(err), zap.Int64("swimLaneID", swimLaneID))
   321→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to fetch updated swim lane", "internal_error")
   322→		return
   323→	}
   324→
   325→	s.logger.Info("Swim lane updated", zap.Int64("swimLaneID", swimLaneID), zap.Int64("projectID", projectID))
   326→	respondJSON(w, http.StatusOK, sl)
   327→}
   328→
   329→// HandleDeleteSwimLane deletes a swim lane
   330→func (s *Server) HandleDeleteSwimLane(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   331→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
   332→	defer cancel()
   333→
   334→	userID := r.Context().Value(UserIDKey).(int64)
   335→	swimLaneID, err := strconv.ParseInt(chi.URLParam(r, "id"), 10, 64)
   336→	if err != nil {
   337→		s.logger.Warn("Invalid swim lane ID", zap.Error(err))
   338→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid swim lane ID", "invalid_input")
   339→		return
   340→	}
   341→
   342→	// Get swim lane's project ID and verify user has access
   343→	var projectID int64
   344→	projectQuery := `SELECT project_id FROM swim_lanes WHERE id = ?`
   345→	if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, projectQuery, swimLaneID).Scan(&projectID); err == sql.ErrNoRows {
   346→		respondError(w, http.StatusNotFound, "swim lane not found", "not_found")
   347→		return
   348→	} else if err != nil {
   349→		s.logger.Error("Failed to get swim lane project", zap.Error(err), zap.Int64("swimLaneID", swimLaneID))
   350→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to get swim lane project", "internal_error")
   351→		return
   352→	}
   353→
   354→	// Verify user has access to the project
   355→	hasAccess, err := s.checkProjectAccess(ctx, userID, projectID)
   356→	if err != nil {
   357→		s.logger.Error("Failed to verify project access", zap.Error(err), zap.Int64("userID", userID), zap.Int64("projectID", projectID))
   358→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to verify project access", "internal_error")
   359→		return
   360→	}
   361→	if !hasAccess {
   362→		s.logger.Warn("Access denied to project", zap.Int64("userID", userID), zap.Int64("projectID", projectID))
   363→		respondError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "access denied", "forbidden")
   364→		return
   365→	}
   366→
   367→	// Check minimum swim lanes (need at least 2)
   368→	var count int
   369→	countQuery := `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM swim_lanes WHERE project_id = ?`
   370→	if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, countQuery, projectID).Scan(&count); err != nil {
   371→		s.logger.Error("Failed to count swim lanes", zap.Error(err), zap.Int64("projectID", projectID))
   372→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to count swim lanes", "internal_error")
   373→		return
   374→	}
   375→	if count <= 2 {
   376→		respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "minimum 2 swim lanes required per project", "min_limit_reached")
   377→		return
   378→	}
   379→
   380→	query := `DELETE FROM swim_lanes WHERE id = ?`
   381→	result, err := s.db.ExecContext(ctx, query, swimLaneID)
   382→	if err != nil {
   383→		s.logger.Error("Failed to delete swim lane", zap.Error(err), zap.Int64("swimLaneID", swimLaneID))
   384→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to delete swim lane", "internal_error")
   385→		return
   386→	}
   387→
   388→	rowsAffected, err := result.RowsAffected()
   389→	if err != nil {
   390→		s.logger.Error("Failed to verify deletion", zap.Error(err))
   391→		respondError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "failed to verify deletion", "internal_error")
   392→		return
   393→	}
   394→
   395→	if rowsAffected == 0 {
   396→		respondError(w, http.StatusNotFound, "swim lane not found", "not_found")
   397→		return
   398→	}
   399→
   400→	s.logger.Info("Swim lane deleted", zap.Int64("swimLaneID", swimLaneID), zap.Int64("projectID", projectID))
   401→	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
   402→}
   403→

data.prompt:
Explore the SprintSpark codebase thoroughly to understand the swim lane / status synchronization logic. The issue is that tasks can end up with mismatched status and swim lane (e.g., status="done" but swim_lane_id pointing to "To Do" column, or vice versa).

Look at:
1. Backend API handlers for task updates (PATCH /api/tasks/:id) - how does it handle swim_lane_id and status fields when one is updated?
2. Backend API handlers for task creation - does it sync status with swim_lane?
3. Frontend code that updates tasks (drag and drop on kanban board, status dropdown changes, swim lane changes)
4. The database schema for tasks and swim_lanes tables
5. The recent commit "feat: fix swim lane/status sync" to see what was already fixed

Focus on finding any code paths where status and swim_lane_id can get out of sync. Be very thorough - check all update paths including drag-and-drop, inline editing, task creation, and API endpoints.

Explore the SprintSpark codebase thoroughly to understand the swim lane / status synchronization logic. The issue is that tasks can end up with mismatched status and swim lane (e.g., status="done" but swim_lane_id pointing to "To Do" column, or vice versa).

Look at:
1. Backend API handlers for task updates (PATCH /api/tasks/:id) - how does it handle swim_lane_id and status fields when one is updated?
2. Backend API handlers for task creation - does it sync status with swim_lane?
3. Frontend code that updates tasks (drag and drop on kanban board, status dropdown changes, swim lane changes)
4. The database schema for tasks and swim_lanes tables
5. The recent commit "feat: fix swim lane/status sync" to see what was already fixed

Focus on finding any code paths where status and swim_lane_id can get out of sync. Be very thorough - check all update paths including drag-and-drop, inline editing, task creation, and API endpoints.
