1→-- Fix tasks where status doesn't match their swim lane's status_category
     2→-- This is a one-time data fix for existing out-of-sync tasks
     3→
     4→UPDATE tasks SET status = (
     5→    SELECT sl.status_category FROM swim_lanes sl WHERE sl.id = tasks.swim_lane_id
     6→)
     7→WHERE swim_lane_id IS NOT NULL
     8→AND status != (SELECT sl.status_category FROM swim_lanes sl WHERE sl.id = tasks.swim_lane_id);
     9→

content.content:
1→-- Add per-project task numbering (like GitHub issues)
     2→-- Each project's tasks are numbered sequentially starting from 1
     3→
     4→ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN task_number INTEGER;
     5→
     6→-- Backfill existing tasks with sequential numbers per project (ordered by creation)
     7→-- SQLite doesn't support UPDATE with window functions directly, so we use a subquery
     8→UPDATE tasks SET task_number = (
     9→    SELECT cnt FROM (
    10→        SELECT id, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY project_id ORDER BY id) AS cnt
    11→        FROM tasks
    12→    ) numbered
    13→    WHERE numbered.id = tasks.id
    14→);
    15→
    16→-- Ensure task_number is unique per project
    17→CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_tasks_project_task_number ON tasks(project_id, task_number);
    18→

1→import { useState, useEffect, useRef, useCallback } from 'react'
     2→import { useParams, useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'
     3→import ReactMarkdown from 'react-markdown'
     4→import remarkGfm from 'remark-gfm'
     5→import Button from '../components/ui/Button'
     6→import { apiClient, Task, type SwimLane, type Sprint, type ProjectMember, type Attachment, type TaskComment } from '../lib/api'
     7→
     8→interface TaskDetailProps {
     9→  isModal?: boolean
    10→  onClose?: () => void
    11→}
    12→
    13→export default function TaskDetail({ isModal, onClose }: TaskDetailProps) {
    14→  const { projectId, taskNumber } = useParams()
    15→  const navigate = useNavigate()
    16→  const [task, setTask] = useState<Task | null>(null)
    17→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
    18→  const [error, setError] = useState('')
    19→  const [saving, setSaving] = useState(false)
    20→
    21→  // Inline editing
    22→  const [editingField, setEditingField] = useState<string | null>(null)
    23→  const [editValue, setEditValue] = useState('')
    24→  const titleRef = useRef<HTMLInputElement>(null)
    25→  const descRef = useRef<HTMLTextAreaElement>(null)
    26→  const commentRef = useRef<HTMLTextAreaElement>(null)
    27→
    28→  // Reference data
    29→  const [sprints, setSprints] = useState<Sprint[]>([])
    30→  const [swimLanes, setSwimLanes] = useState<SwimLane[]>([])
    31→  const [members, setMembers] = useState<ProjectMember[]>([])
    32→
    33→  // Attachments
    34→  const [attachments, setAttachments] = useState<Attachment[]>([])
    35→  const [uploading, setUploading] = useState(false)
    36→  const [editingAltId, setEditingAltId] = useState<number | null>(null)
    37→  const [editingAltValue, setEditingAltValue] = useState('')
    38→  const [pendingFile, setPendingFile] = useState<File | null>(null)
    39→  const fileInputRef = useRef<HTMLInputElement>(null)
    40→
    41→  // Comments
    42→  const [comments, setComments] = useState<TaskComment[]>([])
    43→  const [newComment, setNewComment] = useState('')
    44→  const [postingComment, setPostingComment] = useState(false)
    45→
    46→  // Image picker
    47→  const [imagePickerTarget, setImagePickerTarget] = useState<'description' | 'comment' | null>(null)
    48→
    49→  // Confirm modal
    50→  const [confirmAction, setConfirmAction] = useState<{ message: string; onConfirm: () => void } | null>(null)
    51→
    52→  useEffect(() => {
    53→    loadTask()
    54→    loadSprints()
    55→    loadSwimLanes()
    56→    loadMembers()
    57→  }, [projectId, taskNumber])
    58→
    59→  useEffect(() => {
    60→    if (task?.id) {
    61→      loadComments(task.id)
    62→      loadAttachments(task.id)
    63→    }
    64→  }, [task?.id])
    65→
    66→  useEffect(() => {
    67→    if (editingField === 'title') titleRef.current?.focus()
    68→    if (editingField === 'description') {
    69→      const el = descRef.current
    70→      if (el) {
    71→        el.focus()
    72→        el.setSelectionRange(el.value.length, el.value.length)
    73→      }
    74→    }
    75→  }, [editingField])
    76→
    77→  const loadTask = async () => {
    78→    try {
    79→      setLoading(true)
    80→      const found = await apiClient.getTaskByNumber(Number(projectId), Number(taskNumber))
    81→      setTask(found)
    82→    } catch (err: unknown) {
    83→      setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Failed to load task')
    84→    } finally {
    85→      setLoading(false)
    86→    }
    87→  }
    88→
    89→  const loadSprints = async () => {
    90→    try { setSprints(await apiClient.getSprints()) } catch { /* ignore */ }
    91→  }
    92→
    93→  const loadSwimLanes = async () => {
    94→    try {
    95→      const lanes = await apiClient.getSwimLanes(Number(projectId))
    96→      setSwimLanes(lanes.sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position))
    97→    } catch { /* ignore */ }
    98→  }
    99→
   100→  const loadComments = async (id?: number) => {
   101→    const taskId = id ?? task?.id
   102→    if (!taskId) return
   103→    try { setComments(await apiClient.getTaskComments(taskId)) } catch { /* ignore */ }
   104→  }
   105→
   106→  const loadMembers = async () => {
   107→    try { setMembers(await apiClient.getProjectMembers(Number(projectId))) } catch { /* ignore */ }
   108→  }
   109→
   110→  const loadAttachments = async (id?: number) => {
   111→    const taskId = id ?? task?.id
   112→    if (!taskId) return
   113→    try { setAttachments(await apiClient.getTaskAttachments(taskId)) } catch { /* ignore */ }
   114→  }
   115→
   116→  const handleFileUpload = (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
   117→    const file = e.target.files?.[0]
   118→    if (!file) return
   119→
   120→    const allowedTypes = ['image/', 'video/', 'application/pdf']
   121→    if (!allowedTypes.some(t => file.type.startsWith(t))) {
   122→      setError('Only images, videos, and PDFs are allowed')
   123→      e.target.value = ''
   124→      return
   125→    }
   126→
   127→    setPendingFile(file)
   128→    e.target.value = ''
   129→  }
   130→
   131→  const handleConfirmUpload = async (file: File, altText: string) => {
   132→    try {
   133→      setUploading(true)
   134→      const sig = await apiClient.getUploadSignature()
   135→
   136→      const formData = new FormData()
   137→      formData.append('file', file)
   138→      formData.append('api_key', sig.api_key)
   139→      formData.append('timestamp', String(sig.timestamp))
   140→      formData.append('signature', sig.signature)
   141→
   142→      const uploadRes = await fetch(
   143→        `https://api.cloudinary.com/v1_1/${sig.cloud_name}/auto/upload`,
   144→        { method: 'POST', body: formData }
   145→      )
   146→
   147→      if (!uploadRes.ok) throw new Error('Upload to Cloudinary failed')
   148→      const uploadData = await uploadRes.json()
   149→
   150→      let fileType = 'image'
   151→      if (file.type.startsWith('video/')) fileType = 'video'
   152→      else if (file.type === 'application/pdf') fileType = 'pdf'
   153→
   154→      await apiClient.createTaskAttachment(task!.id!, {
   155→        filename: file.name,
   156→        alt_name: altText,
   157→        file_type: fileType,
   158→        content_type: file.type,
   159→        file_size: file.size,
   160→        cloudinary_url: uploadData.secure_url,
   161→        cloudinary_public_id: uploadData.public_id,
   162→      })
   163→
   164→      await loadAttachments()
   165→    } catch (err: unknown) {
   166→      setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Failed to upload file')
   167→    } finally {
   168→      setUploading(false)
   169→      setPendingFile(null)
   170→    }
   171→  }
   172→
   173→  const handleDeleteAttachment = (attachmentId: number) => {
   174→    setConfirmAction({
   175→      message: 'Are you sure you want to delete this attachment? This cannot be undone.',
   176→      onConfirm: async () => {
   177→        try {
   178→          await apiClient.deleteTaskAttachment(task!.id!, attachmentId)
   179→          await loadAttachments()
   180→        } catch (err: unknown) {
   181→          setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Failed to delete attachment')
   182→        }
   183→        setConfirmAction(null)
   184→      },
   185→    })
   186→  }
   187→
   188→  const handleSaveAltName = async (attachmentId: number) => {
   189→    try {
   190→      await apiClient.updateAttachment(attachmentId, { alt_name: editingAltValue })
   191→      setEditingAltId(null)
   192→      await loadAttachments()
   193→    } catch (err: unknown) {
   194→      setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Failed to update alt name')
   195→    }
   196→  }
   197→
   198→  const insertImageMarkdown = useCallback((alt: string, url: string) => {
   199→    const markdown = `![${alt}](${url})`
   200→

data.prompt:
Explore the SprintSpark codebase thoroughly to understand the swim lane / status synchronization logic. The issue is that tasks can end up with mismatched status and swim lane (e.g., status="done" but swim_lane_id pointing to "To Do" column, or vice versa).

Look at:
1. Backend API handlers for task updates (PATCH /api/tasks/:id) - how does it handle swim_lane_id and status fields when one is updated?
2. Backend API handlers for task creation - does it sync status with swim_lane?
3. Frontend code that updates tasks (drag and drop on kanban board, status dropdown changes, swim lane changes)
4. The database schema for tasks and swim_lanes tables
5. The recent commit "feat: fix swim lane/status sync" to see what was already fixed

Focus on finding any code paths where status and swim_lane_id can get out of sync. Be very thorough - check all update paths including drag-and-drop, inline editing, task creation, and API endpoints.

    17→func TestHandleListTasks(t *testing.T) {

    31→	)

    34→	}
    35→
    36→	_, err = ts.DB.ExecContext(ctx,

    39→	)

    44→	rec, req := ts.MakeAuthRequest(t, http.MethodGet, "/api/projects/1/tasks", nil, userID,

    47→	ts.HandleListTasks(rec, req)

    72→func TestHandleCreateTask(t *testing.T) {

    75→
    76→	userID := ts.CreateTestUser(t, "test@example.com", "password123")

    79→	taskData := CreateTaskRequest{

    83→		Priority:    stringPtr("urgent"),
    84→	}
    85→
    86→	rec, req := ts.MakeAuthRequest(t, http.MethodPost, "/api/projects/1/tasks", taskData, userID,

    89→	ts.HandleCreateTask(rec, req)

    95→	var createdTask Task

    98→	if createdTask.Title != "New Task" {

   100→	}
   101→

   104→	}
   105→
   106→	if createdTask.Description == nil || *createdTask.Description != "Task description with **markdown**" {
   107→		t.Errorf("Expected description with markdown")
   108→	}
   109→}

   111→func TestHandleCreateTaskValidation(t *testing.T) {
   112→	tests := []struct {

   115→		wantStatus int

   123→		},
   124→		{
   125→			name:       "invalid status",

   127→			wantStatus: http.StatusBadRequest,
   128→			wantError:  "invalid status",
   129→		},

   133→			wantStatus: http.StatusBadRequest,
   134→			wantError:  "invalid priority",
   135→		},
   136→	}

   138→	for _, tt := range tests {

   149→			ts.HandleCreateTask(rec, req)

   154→	}

   313→
   314→	tagResult2, _ := ts.DB.ExecContext(ctx, "INSERT INTO tags (user_id, name, color) VALUES (?, ?, ?)", userID, "feature", "#00FF00")
   315→	tagID2, _ := tagResult2.LastInsertId()
   316→
   317→	// Create task
   318→	taskID := ts.CreateTestTask(t, projectID, "Tagged Task")
   319→

1→-- Initial schema for SprintSpark
     2→
     3→-- Users table
     4→CREATE TABLE users (
     5→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
     6→    email TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
     7→    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
     8→    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
     9→    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
    10→);
    11→
    12→CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON users(email);
    13→
    14→-- Projects table
    15→CREATE TABLE projects (
    16→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    17→    owner_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    18→    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    19→    description TEXT,
    20→    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    21→    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    22→    FOREIGN KEY (owner_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
    23→);
    24→
    25→CREATE INDEX idx_projects_owner_id ON projects(owner_id);
    26→
    27→-- Tasks table
    28→CREATE TABLE tasks (
    29→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    30→    project_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    31→    title TEXT NOT NULL,
    32→    description TEXT,
    33→    status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('todo', 'in_progress', 'done')) DEFAULT 'todo',
    34→    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    35→    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    36→    FOREIGN KEY (project_id) REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
    37→);
    38→
    39→CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_project_id ON tasks(project_id);
    40→CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_status ON tasks(status);
    41→
    42→-- Trigger to update updated_at timestamp for users
    43→CREATE TRIGGER update_users_timestamp
    44→AFTER UPDATE ON users
    45→FOR EACH ROW
    46→BEGIN
    47→    UPDATE users SET updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = OLD.id;
    48→END;
    49→
    50→-- Trigger to update updated_at timestamp for projects
    51→CREATE TRIGGER update_projects_timestamp
    52→AFTER UPDATE ON projects
    53→FOR EACH ROW
    54→BEGIN
    55→    UPDATE projects SET updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = OLD.id;
    56→END;
    57→
    58→-- Trigger to update updated_at timestamp for tasks
    59→CREATE TRIGGER update_tasks_timestamp
    60→AFTER UPDATE ON tasks
    61→FOR EACH ROW
    62→BEGIN
    63→    UPDATE tasks SET updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = OLD.id;
    64→END;
    65→

1→-- Add swim lanes table for customizable task statuses per project
     2→
     3→-- Swim lanes define the columns in the kanban board for each project
     4→CREATE TABLE swim_lanes (
     5→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
     6→    project_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
     7→    name TEXT NOT NULL,
     8→    color TEXT NOT NULL, -- hex color for the swim lane indicator
     9→    position INTEGER NOT NULL, -- order of columns (0-based)
    10→    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    11→    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    12→    FOREIGN KEY (project_id) REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
    13→);
    14→
    15→CREATE INDEX idx_swim_lanes_project_id ON swim_lanes(project_id);
    16→CREATE INDEX idx_swim_lanes_position ON swim_lanes(project_id, position);
    17→
    18→-- Ensure unique positions per project
    19→CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_swim_lanes_unique_position ON swim_lanes(project_id, position);
    20→
    21→-- Trigger to update updated_at timestamp for swim_lanes
    22→CREATE TRIGGER update_swim_lanes_timestamp
    23→AFTER UPDATE ON swim_lanes
    24→FOR EACH ROW
    25→BEGIN
    26→    UPDATE swim_lanes SET updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = OLD.id;
    27→END;
    28→
    29→-- Update tasks table to reference swim_lane_id instead of hardcoded status
    30→-- First, add the new column
    31→ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN swim_lane_id INTEGER REFERENCES swim_lanes(id) ON DELETE SET NULL;
    32→
    33→-- Create index for the new column
    34→CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_swim_lane_id ON tasks(swim_lane_id);
    35→
    36→-- Migrate existing projects to have default swim lanes
    37→-- This will be done in a separate data migration after the schema is updated
    38→

1→package api
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"fmt"
     6→	"net/http"
     7→	"strings"
     8→	"testing"
     9→	"time"
    10→)
    11→
    12→// createDefaultSwimLanes inserts the 3 default swim lanes for a test project
    13→// and returns their IDs in order [To Do, In Progress, Done].
    14→func createDefaultSwimLanes(t *testing.T, ts *TestServer, projectID int64) [3]int64 {
    15→	t.Helper()
    16→
    17→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
    18→	defer cancel()
    19→
    20→	defaults := []struct {
    21→		name           string
    22→		color          string
    23→		position       int
    24→		statusCategory string
    25→	}{
    26→		{"To Do", "#6B7280", 0, "todo"},
    27→		{"In Progress", "#3B82F6", 1, "in_progress"},
    28→		{"Done", "#10B981", 2, "done"},
    29→	}
    30→
    31→	var ids [3]int64
    32→	for i, sl := range defaults {
    33→		result, err := ts.DB.ExecContext(ctx,
    34→			`INSERT INTO swim_lanes (project_id, name, color, position, status_category) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
    35→			projectID, sl.name, sl.color, sl.position, sl.statusCategory,
    36→		)
    37→		if err != nil {
    38→			t.Fatalf("Failed to create swim lane %q: %v", sl.name, err)
    39→		}
    40→		id, err := result.LastInsertId()
    41→		if err != nil {
    42→			t.Fatalf("Failed to get swim lane ID: %v", err)
    43→		}
    44→		ids[i] = id
    45→	}
    46→	return ids
    47→}
    48→
    49→func TestHandleListSwimLanes(t *testing.T) {
    50→	ts := NewTestServer(t)
    51→	defer ts.Close()
    52→
    53→	userID := ts.CreateTestUser(t, "test@example.com", "password123")
    54→	projectID := ts.CreateTestProject(t, userID, "Test Project")
    55→	createDefaultSwimLanes(t, ts, projectID)
    56→
    57→	rec, req := ts.MakeAuthRequest(t, http.MethodGet, fmt.Sprintf("/api/projects/%d/swim-lanes", projectID), nil, userID,
    58→		map[string]string{"projectId": fmt.Sprintf("%d", projectID)})
    59→
    60→	ts.HandleListSwimLanes(rec, req)
    61→
    62→	AssertStatusCode(t, rec.Code, http.StatusOK)
    63→
    64→	var lanes []SwimLane
    65→	DecodeJSON(t, rec, &lanes)
    66→
    67→	if len(lanes) != 3 {
    68→		t.Fatalf("Expected 3 swim lanes, got %d", len(lanes))
    69→	}
    70→
    71→	// Verify they come back ordered by position
    72→	expectedNames := []string{"To Do", "In Progress", "Done"}
    73→	for i, name := range expectedNames {
    74→		if lanes[i].Name != name {
    75→			t.Errorf("Lane %d: expected name %q, got %q", i, name, lanes[i].Name)
    76→		}
    77→		if lanes[i].Position != i {
    78→			t.Errorf("Lane %d: expected position %d, got %d", i, i, lanes[i].Position)
    79→		}
    80→		if lanes[i].ProjectID != projectID {
    81→			t.Errorf("Lane %d: expected project_id %d, got %d", i, projectID, lanes[i].ProjectID)
    82→		}
    83→	}
    84→}
    85→
    86→func TestHandleCreateSwimLane(t *testing.T) {
    87→	ts := NewTestServer(t)
    88→	defer ts.Close()
    89→
    90→	userID := ts.CreateTestUser(t, "test@example.com", "password123")
    91→	projectID := ts.CreateTestProject(t, userID, "Test Project")
    92→	createDefaultSwimLanes(t, ts, projectID)
    93→
    94→	body := CreateSwimLaneRequest{
    95→		Name:           "Review",
    96→		Color:          "#F59E0B",
    97→		Position:       3,
    98→		StatusCategory: "in_progress",
    99→	}
   100→
   101→	rec, req := ts.MakeAuthRequest(t, http.MethodPost, fmt.Sprintf("/api/projects/%d/swim-lanes", projectID), body, userID,
   102→		map[string]string{"projectId": fmt.Sprintf("%d", projectID)})
   103→
   104→	ts.HandleCreateSwimLane(rec, req)
   105→
   106→	AssertStatusCode(t, rec.Code, http.StatusCreated)
   107→
   108→	var lane SwimLane
   109→	DecodeJSON(t, rec, &lane)
   110→
   111→	if lane.Name != "Review" {
   112→		t.Errorf("Expected name 'Review', got %q", lane.Name)
   113→	}
   114→	if lane.Color != "#F59E0B" {
   115→		t.Errorf("Expected color '#F59E0B', got %q", lane.Color)
   116→	}
   117→	if lane.Position != 3 {
   118→		t.Errorf("Expected position 3, got %d", lane.Position)
   119→	}
   120→	if lane.StatusCategory != "in_progress" {
   121→		t.Errorf("Expected status_category 'in_progress', got %q", lane.StatusCategory)
   122→	}
   123→	if lane.ProjectID != projectID {
   124→		t.Errorf("Expected project_id %d, got %d", projectID, lane.ProjectID)
   125→	}
   126→	if lane.ID == 0 {
   127→		t.Error("Expected non-zero swim lane ID")
   128→	}
   129→}
   130→
   131→func TestHandleCreateSwimLaneDefaultColor(t *testing.T) {
   132→	ts := NewTestServer(t)
   133→	defer ts.Close()
   134→
   135→	userID := ts.CreateTestUser(t, "test@example.com", "password123")
   136→	projectID := ts.CreateTestProject(t, userID, "Test Project")
   137→
   138→	body := CreateSwimLaneRequest{
   139→		Name:           "Backlog",
   140→		Position:       0,
   141→		StatusCategory: "todo",
   142→	}
   143→
   144→	rec, req := ts.MakeAuthRequest(t, http.MethodPost, fmt.Sprintf("/api/projects/%d/swim-lanes", projectID), body, userID,
   145→		map[string]string{"projectId": fmt.Sprintf("%d", projectID)})
   146→
   147→	ts.HandleCreateSwimLane(rec, req)
   148→
   149→	AssertStatusCode(t, rec.Code, http.StatusCreated)
   150→
   151→	var lane SwimLane
   152→	DecodeJSON(t, rec, &lane)
   153→
   154→	if lane.Color != "#6B7280" {
   155→		t.Errorf("Expected default color '#6B7280', got %q", lane.Color)
   156→	}
   157→}
   158→
   159→func TestHandleCreateSwimLaneValidation(t *testing.T) {
   160→	tests := []struct {
   161→		name       string
   162→		body       CreateSwimLaneRequest
   163→		wantStatus int
   164→		wantError  string
   165→	}{
   166→		{
   167→			name:       "missing name",
   168→			body:       CreateSwimLaneRequest{Name: "", Color: "#FF0000", Position: 0, StatusCategory: "todo"},
   169→			wantStatus: http.StatusBadRequest,
   170→			wantError:  "swim lane name is required",
   171→		},
   172→		{
   173→			name:       "name too long",
   174→			body:       CreateSwimLaneRequest{Name: strings.Repeat("x", 51), Color: "#FF0000", Position: 0, StatusCategory: "todo"},
   175→			wantStatus: http.StatusBadRequest,
   176→			wantError:  "swim lane name is too long",
   177→		},
   178→		{
   179→			name:       "missing status_category",
   180→			body:       CreateSwimLaneRequest{Name: "Valid", Color: "#FF0000", Position: 0},
   181→			wantStatus: http.StatusBadRequest,
   182→			wantError:  "status_category is required",
   183→		},
   184→		{
   185→			name:       "invalid status_category",
   186→			body:       CreateSwimLaneRequest{Name: "Valid", Color: "#FF0000", Position: 0, StatusCategory: "invalid"},
   187→			wantStatus: http.StatusBadRequest,
   188→			wantError:  "invalid status_category",
   189→		},
   190→	}
   191→
   192→	for _, tt := range tests {
   193→		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   194→			ts := NewTestServer(t)
   195→			defer ts.Close()
   196→
   197→			userID := ts.CreateTestUser(t, "test@example.com", "password123")
   198→			projectID := ts.CreateTestProject(t, userID, "Test Project")
   199→
   200→			rec, req := ts.MakeAuthRequest(t, http.MethodPost, fmt.Sprintf("/api/projects/%d/swim-lanes", projectID), tt.body, userID,
   201→				map[string]string{"projectId": fmt.Sprintf("%d", projectID)})
   202→
   203→			ts.HandleCreateSwimLane(rec, req)
   204→
   205→			AssertError(t, rec, tt.wantStatus, tt.wantError, "invalid_input")
   206→		})
   207→	}
   208→}
   209→
   210→func TestHandleCreateSwimLaneMaxLimit(t *testing.T) {
   211→	ts := NewTestServer(t)
   212→	defer ts.Close()
   213→
   214→	userID := ts.CreateTestUser(t, "test@example.com", "password123")
   215→	projectID := ts.CreateTestProject(t, userID, "Test Project")
   216→
   217→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
   218→	defer cancel()
   219→
   220→	// Create 6 swim lanes (the maximum)
   221→	for i := 0; i < 6; i++ {
   222→		_, err := ts.DB.ExecContext(ctx,
   223→			`INSERT INTO swim_lanes (project_id, name, color, position, status_category) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
   224→			projectID, fmt.Sprintf("Lane %d", i), "#AABBCC", i, "todo",
   225→		)
   226→		if err != nil {
   227→			t.Fatalf("Failed to create swim lane %d: %v", i, err)
   228→		}
   229→	}
   230→
   231→	body := CreateSwimLaneRequest{
   232→		Name:           "Seventh Lane",
   233→		Color:          "#FF0000",
   234→		Position:       6,
   235→		StatusCategory: "todo",
   236→	}

   381→func TestHandleDeleteSwimLaneMinimumLimit(t *testing.T) {
   382→	ts := NewTestServer(t)
   383→	defer ts.Close()
   384→
   385→	userID := ts.CreateTestUser(t, "test@example.com", "password123")
   386→	projectID := ts.CreateTestProject(t, userID, "Test Project")
   387→
   388→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
   389→	defer cancel()
   390→
   391→	// Create exactly 2 swim lanes (the minimum)
   392→	result1, err := ts.DB.ExecContext(ctx,
   393→		`INSERT INTO swim_lanes (project_id, name, color, position, status_category) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
   394→		projectID, "To Do", "#6B7280", 0, "todo",
   395→	)
   396→	if err != nil {
   397→		t.Fatalf("Failed to create swim lane: %v", err)
   398→	}
   399→	laneID, err := result1.LastInsertId()
   400→	if err != nil {
   401→		t.Fatalf("Failed to get swim lane ID: %v", err)
   402→	}
   403→
   404→	_, err = ts.DB.ExecContext(ctx,
   405→		`INSERT INTO swim_lanes (project_id, name, color, position, status_category) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
   406→		projectID, "Done", "#10B981", 1, "done",
   407→	)
   408→	if err != nil {
   409→		t.Fatalf("Failed to create swim lane: %v", err)
   410→	}
   411→
   412→	// Try to delete one of the 2 remaining lanes (should fail)
   413→	rec, req := ts.MakeAuthRequest(t, http.MethodDelete, fmt.Sprintf("/api/swim-lanes/%d", laneID), nil, userID,
   414→		map[string]string{"id": fmt.Sprintf("%d", laneID)})
   415→
   416→	ts.HandleDeleteSwimLane(rec, req)
   417→
   418→	AssertError(t, rec, http.StatusBadRequest, "minimum 2 swim lanes required", "min_limit_reached")
   419→}

data.prompt:
Explore the SprintSpark codebase thoroughly to understand the swim lane / status synchronization logic. The issue is that tasks can end up with mismatched status and swim lane (e.g., status="done" but swim_lane_id pointing to "To Do" column, or vice versa).

Look at:
1. Backend API handlers for task updates (PATCH /api/tasks/:id) - how does it handle swim_lane_id and status fields when one is updated?
2. Backend API handlers for task creation - does it sync status with swim_lane?
3. Frontend code that updates tasks (drag and drop on kanban board, status dropdown changes, swim lane changes)
4. The database schema for tasks and swim_lanes tables
5. The recent commit "feat: fix swim lane/status sync" to see what was already fixed

Focus on finding any code paths where status and swim_lane_id can get out of sync. Be very thorough - check all update paths including drag-and-drop, inline editing, task creation, and API endpoints.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a complete understanding. Let me write a comprehensive analysis document.

## Swim Lane/Status Synchronization Analysis

Based on my thorough exploration of the SprintSpark codebase, here's a complete analysis of the swim lane/status synchronization logic:

---

## Current Implementation (After Commit 3d4c88e)

### Database Schema
- **Migration 021** (`add_swim_lane_status_category.sql`): Added `status_category` column to `swim_lanes` table with values: `todo`, `in_progress`, or `done`
- **Migration 022** (`fix_task_status_sync.sql`): One-time data fix to update tasks where status didn't match their swim lane's `status_category`
- **Migration 023** (`add_task_number.sql`): Added per-project task numbering for better UX

The `tasks` table has:
- `status` column (values: todo, in_progress, done)
- `swim_lane_id` column (nullable foreign key to swim_lanes)

### Synchronization Logic

#### **Backend: Task Creation (HandleCreateTask)**
File: `/api/internal/api/task_handlers.go` (lines 180-360)

**Sync Strategy:**
1. If `swim_lane_id` is provided in request:
   - Query the swim lane's `status_category`
   - **Override** any provided `status` with the category value
   - Task inserted with derived status

2. If only `status` is provided (no `swim_lane_id`):
   - Validate the status value (must be: todo, in_progress, done)
   - **Auto-find** first swim lane with matching `status_category`
   - Task inserted with that `swim_lane_id`

3. If neither is provided:
   - Default `status = "todo"`
   - Find first swim lane with `status_category = "todo"`
   - Task inserted with that `swim_lane_id`

**Key Code Block (lines 231-248):**
```go
var swimLaneID *int64
if req.SwimLaneID != nil {
    swimLaneID = req.SwimLaneID
    // Derive status from swim lane's status_category
    var category string
    laneQuery := `SELECT status_category FROM swim_lanes WHERE id = ? AND project_id = ?`
    if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, laneQuery, *req.SwimLaneID, projectID).Scan(&category); err == nil && category != "" {
        status = category
    }
} else {
    // Find first swim lane matching the status category
    var laneID int64
    laneQuery := `SELECT id FROM swim_lanes WHERE project_id = ? AND status_category = ? ORDER BY position ASC LIMIT 1`
    if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, laneQuery, projectID, status).Scan(&laneID); err == nil {
        swimLaneID = &laneID
    }
}
```

#### **Backend: Task Update (HandleUpdateTask)**
File: `/api/internal/api/task_handlers.go` (lines 362-560)

**Sync Strategy** - Three scenarios:

1. **Only `swim_lane_id` updated** (req.SwimLaneID != nil && req.Status == nil):
   - Query swim lane's `status_category`
   - Update both `swim_lane_id` AND `status` to the category
   - Code (lines 425-434)

2. **Only `status` updated** (req.Status != nil && req.SwimLaneID == nil):
   - Validate the status value
   - **Auto-find** first swim lane with matching `status_category`
   - Update both `status` AND `swim_lane_id` if lane found
   - Code (lines 435-448)

3. **Both provided** (req.Status != nil && req.SwimLaneID != nil):
   - **Trust the swim_lane_id** - it's the source of truth
   - Query swim lane's `status_category`
   - Update both to swim lane's category (ignoring provided status)
   - Code (lines 449-465)

**Critical Code Block (lines 424-466):**
```go
if req.SwimLaneID != nil && req.Status == nil {
    // Swim lane changed — derive status from lane's status_category
    var category string
    laneQuery := `SELECT status_category FROM swim_lanes WHERE id = ? AND project_id = ?`
    if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, laneQuery, *req.SwimLaneID, projectID).Scan(&category); err == nil && category != "" {
        query += ", status = ?"
        args = append(args, category)
    }
    query += ", swim_lane_id = ?"
    args = append(args, *req.SwimLaneID)
} else if req.Status != nil && req.SwimLaneID == nil {
    // Status changed — find first swim lane with matching status_category
    if *req.Status != "todo" && *req.Status != "in_progress" && *req.Status != "done" {
        respondError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid status (must be: todo, in_progress, or done)", "invalid_input")
        return
    }
    query += ", status = ?"
    args = append(args, *req.Status)
    var laneID int64
    laneQuery := `SELECT id FROM swim_lanes WHERE project_id = ? AND status_category = ? ORDER BY position ASC LIMIT 1`
    if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, laneQuery, projectID, *req.Status).Scan(&laneID); err == nil {
        query += ", swim_lane_id = ?"
        args = append(args, laneID)
    }
} else if req.Status != nil && req.SwimLaneID != nil {
    // Both provided — trust swim_lane_id, derive status from it
    var category string
    laneQuery := `SELECT status_category FROM swim_lanes WHERE id = ? AND project_id = ?`
    if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, laneQuery, *req.SwimLaneID, projectID).Scan(&category); err == nil && category != "" {
        query += ", status = ?"
        args = append(args, category)
    } else {
        query += ", status = ?"
        args = append(args, *req.Status)
    }
    query += ", swim_lane_id = ?"
    args = append(args, *req.SwimLaneID)
}
```

#### **Frontend: Drag-and-Drop**
File: `/web/src/routes/ProjectDetail.tsx` (lines 112-136)

When a task is dragged to a new swim lane:
- Only `swim_lane_id` is sent to the backend
- Backend automatically derives and syncs the status
- No direct status modification from frontend

**Code:**
```typescript
const handleDragEnd = async (event: DragEndEvent) => {
    const { active, over } = event
    setActiveTask(null)

    if (!over) return

    const taskId = active.id as number
    const newSwimLaneId = Number(over.id)

    const task = tasks.find(t => t.id === taskId)
    if (!task || task.swim_lane_id === newSwimLaneId) return

    try {
      // Backend auto-syncs status from swim lane's status_category
      await updateTask(taskId, {
        swim_lane_id: newSwimLaneId,
      })
    } catch (err) {
      alert(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Failed to update task status')
    }
}
```

#### **Frontend: Task Creation**
File: `/web/src/routes/ProjectDetail.tsx` (lines 83-105)

New tasks default to first swim lane (which has `status_category = "todo"`):
```typescript
const handleCreateTask = async () => {
    if (!newTaskTitle.trim() || !projectId) return

    try {
      setCreating(true)
      await createTask({
        title: newTaskTitle.trim(),
        description: newTaskDescription.trim() || undefined,
        status: 'todo',
        swim_lane_id: swimLanes.length > 0 ? swimLanes[0].id : undefined,
        due_date: newTaskDueDate || undefined,
      })
      // ... rest of code
    }
}
```

#### **Frontend: Task Detail View**
File: `/web/src/routes/TaskDetail.tsx` (lines 245-248)

When updating swim lane from task detail:
- Only `swim_lane_id` is sent
- Backend syncs status automatically
- No direct status field update path in UI

---

## Potential Sync Issues & Edge Cases

### **Issue 1: Missing Swim Lane**
**Scenario:** When a task is created with a `status` but NO swim lane exists with matching `status_category`

**Current Behavior (line 245):**
```go
if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, laneQuery, projectID, status).Scan(&laneID); err == nil {
    swimLaneID = &laneID
}
```
- Error is silently ignored (`if err == nil`)
- `swimLaneID` remains nil
- **Task is created with status but swim_lane_id = NULL**
- This creates a mismatch: status is valid but orphaned from any lane

**Risk:** A project could have all swim lanes deleted or misconfigured with wrong status_categories. New tasks would have status but no lane.

---

### **Issue 2: Swim Lane Category Mismatch During Update**
**Scenario:** A swim lane's `status_category` is queried but returns empty string

**Current Behavior (lines 428-432):**
```go
if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, laneQuery, *req.SwimLaneID, projectID).Scan(&category); err == nil && category != "" {
    query += ", status = ?"
    args = append(args, category)
}
query += ", swim_lane_id = ?"
args = append(args, *req.SwimLaneID)
```
- If `category` is empty string, the `if` block is skipped
- Only `swim_lane_id` is updated, NOT `status`
- **Task gets new swim_lane_id but status remains unchanged**
- Creates mismatch if old status wasn't matching the new lane's category

**Risk:** Corrupted swim_lane data (status_category = "") would cause desynchronization.

---

### **Issue 3: Swim Lane ID Points to Different Project**
**Scenario:** Request includes a `swim_lane_id` from a different project

**Current Behavior (line 428):**
```go
laneQuery := `SELECT status_category FROM swim_lanes WHERE id = ? AND project_id = ?`
```
- The query correctly checks `project_id`
- If lane doesn't exist or is from different project, error is silently caught
- Same as Issue 1 - task created without lane assignment

**Risk:** Frontend could maliciously or accidentally send wrong lane IDs.

---

### **Issue 4: Race Condition During Swim Lane Update**
**Scenario:** A swim lane's `status_category` is changed AFTER backend reads it but BEFORE task is updated

**Current Behavior:** 
- Backend reads category value
- Task updated with that category value as status
- Even if lane's category changed, task uses stale value
- Tasks ending up with status that doesn't match lane's current category

**Risk:** Rare but possible in high-concurrency scenarios.

---

## What Code Paths DON'T Have Sync Issues

✅ **Drag-and-Drop** - Always sends only `swim_lane_id`, backend auto-syncs  
✅ **Task Detail Status Change** - Sends only `swim_lane_id`, backend auto-syncs  
✅ **Task Creation with Lane** - Derives status from lane category  
✅ **Data Migration** - Migration 022 fixed all existing out-of-sync tasks  

---

## Summary

The current implementation is **mostly solid** with proper synchronization logic at the backend level:

**Strengths:**
- Single source of truth: `swim_lane_id` determines correct status
- Status field is auto-derived from swim lane's category
- Frontend drag-and-drop correctly sends only swim_lane_id
- Migrations fixed historical data

**Weaknesses:**
- Silent error handling when swim lanes don't exist (lines 238, 245, 445)
- Missing validation that swim lane's status_category is not empty
- No explicit constraints preventing status/swim_lane mismatch at DB level
- No warning if swim lane is from different project

**Most Vulnerable Paths:**
1. Task creation without explicit swim_lane_id when status is provided
2. Updating swim lane's status_category after task references it
3. Direct database manipulation bypassing application logic

All files analyzed:
- `/api/internal/api/task_handlers.go` - Core sync logic
- `/api/internal/api/swim_lane_handlers.go` - Swim lane management
- `/web/src/routes/ProjectDetail.tsx` - Frontend drag/drop
- `/web/src/routes/TaskDetail.tsx` - Task detail view
- `/api/internal/db/migrations/021-023` - Schema and data fixes

```go
    if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, laneQuery, *req.SwimLaneID, projectID).Scan(&category); err == nil && category != "" {
        query += ", status = ?"
        args = append(args, category)
    } else {
        query += ", status = ?"
        args = append(args, *req.Status)
    }
    query += ", swim_lane_id = ?"
    args = append(args, *req.SwimLaneID)
}
```

#### **Frontend: Drag-and-Drop**
File: `/web/src/routes/ProjectDetail.tsx` (lines 112-136)

When a task is dragged to a new swim lane:
- Only `swim_lane_id` is sent to the backend
- Backend automatically derives and syncs the status
- No direct status modification from frontend

**Code:**
```typescript
const handleDragEnd = async (event: DragEndEvent) => {
    const { active, over } = event
    setActiveTask(null)

    if (!over) return

    const taskId = active.id as number
    const newSwimLaneId = Number(over.id)

    const task = tasks.find(t => t.id === taskId)
    if (!task || task.swim_lane_id === newSwimLaneId) return

    try {
      // Backend auto-syncs status from swim lane's status_category
      await updateTask(taskId, {
        swim_lane_id: newSwimLaneId,
      })
    } catch (err) {
      alert(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Failed to update task status')
    }
}
```

#### **Frontend: Task Creation**
File: `/web/src/routes/ProjectDetail.tsx` (lines 83-105)

New tasks default to first swim lane (which has `status_category = "todo"`):
```typescript
const handleCreateTask = async () => {
    if (!newTaskTitle.trim() || !projectId) return

    try {
      setCreating(true)
      await createTask({
        title: newTaskTitle.trim(),
        description: newTaskDescription.trim() || undefined,
        status: 'todo',
        swim_lane_id: swimLanes.length > 0 ? swimLanes[0].id : undefined,
        due_date: newTaskDueDate || undefined,
      })
      // ... rest of code
    }
}
```

#### **Frontend: Task Detail View**
File: `/web/src/routes/TaskDetail.tsx` (lines 245-248)

When updating swim lane from task detail:
- Only `swim_lane_id` is sent
- Backend syncs status automatically
- No direct status field update path in UI

---

## Potential Sync Issues & Edge Cases

### **Issue 1: Missing Swim Lane**
**Scenario:** When a task is created with a `status` but NO swim lane exists with matching `status_category`

**Current Behavior (line 245):**
```go
if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, laneQuery, projectID, status).Scan(&laneID); err == nil {
    swimLaneID = &laneID
}
```
- Error is silently ignored (`if err == nil`)
- `swimLaneID` remains nil
- **Task is created with status but swim_lane_id = NULL**
- This creates a mismatch: status is valid but orphaned from any lane

**Risk:** A project could have all swim lanes deleted or misconfigured with wrong status_categories. New tasks would have status but no lane.

---

### **Issue 2: Swim Lane Category Mismatch During Update**
**Scenario:** A swim lane's `status_category` is queried but returns empty string

**Current Behavior (lines 428-432):**
```go
if err := s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, laneQuery, *req.SwimLaneID, projectID).Scan(&category); err == nil && category != "" {
    query += ", status = ?"
    args = append(args, category)
}
query += ", swim_lane_id = ?"
args = append(args, *req.SwimLaneID)
```
- If `category` is empty string, the `if` block is skipped
- Only `swim_lane_id` is updated, NOT `status`
- **Task gets new swim_lane_id but status remains unchanged**
- Creates mismatch if old status wasn't matching the new lane's category

**Risk:** Corrupted swim_lane data (status_category = "") would cause desynchronization.

---

### **Issue 3: Swim Lane ID Points to Different Project**
**Scenario:** Request includes a `swim_lane_id` from a different project

**Current Behavior (line 428):**
```go
laneQuery := `SELECT status_category FROM swim_lanes WHERE id = ? AND project_id = ?`
```
- The query correctly checks `project_id`
- If lane doesn't exist or is from different project, error is silently caught
- Same as Issue 1 - task created without lane assignment

**Risk:** Frontend could maliciously or accidentally send wrong lane IDs.

---

### **Issue 4: Race Condition During Swim Lane Update**
**Scenario:** A swim lane's `status_category` is changed AFTER backend reads it but BEFORE task is updated

**Current Behavior:** 
- Backend reads category value
- Task updated with that category value as status
- Even if lane's category changed, task uses stale value
- Tasks ending up with status that doesn't match lane's current category

**Risk:** Rare but possible in high-concurrency scenarios.

---

## What Code Paths DON'T Have Sync Issues

✅ **Drag-and-Drop** - Always sends only `swim_lane_id`, backend auto-syncs  
✅ **Task Detail Status Change** - Sends only `swim_lane_id`, backend auto-syncs  
✅ **Task Creation with Lane** - Derives status from lane category  
✅ **Data Migration** - Migration 022 fixed all existing out-of-sync tasks  

---

## Summary

The current implementation is **mostly solid** with proper synchronization logic at the backend level:

**Strengths:**
- Single source of truth: `swim_lane_id` determines correct status
- Status field is auto-derived from swim lane's category
- Frontend drag-and-drop correctly sends only swim_lane_id
- Migrations fixed historical data

**Weaknesses:**
- Silent error handling when swim lanes don't exist (lines 238, 245, 445)
- Missing validation that swim lane's status_category is not empty
- No explicit constraints preventing status/swim_lane mismatch at DB level
- No warning if swim lane is from different project

**Most Vulnerable Paths:**
1. Task creation without explicit swim_lane_id when status is provided
2. Updating swim lane's status_category after task references it
3. Direct database manipulation bypassing application logic

All files analyzed:
- `/api/internal/api/task_handlers.go` - Core sync logic
- `/api/internal/api/swim_lane_handlers.go` - Swim lane management
- `/web/src/routes/ProjectDetail.tsx` - Frontend drag/drop
- `/web/src/routes/TaskDetail.tsx` - Task detail view
- `/api/internal/db/migrations/021-023` - Schema and data fixes

1→/**
     2→ * useLocalTasks Hook
     3→ * React hook for managing tasks with local-first optimistic updates
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { useState, useEffect } from 'react'
     7→import { useSync } from '../state/SyncContext'
     8→import { api } from '../lib/api'
     9→import type { TaskDocument } from '../lib/db/schema'
    10→import type { UpdateTaskRequest } from '../lib/api'
    11→
    12→export function useLocalTasks(projectId: number) {
    13→  const { db, syncService } = useSync()
    14→  const [tasks, setTasks] = useState<TaskDocument[]>([])
    15→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
    16→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null)
    17→
    18→  // Subscribe to tasks from local database OR fetch from server
    19→  useEffect(() => {
    20→    if (!db) {
    21→      // Fallback to server-side fetch when RxDB is disabled
    22→      const fetchTasks = async () => {
    23→        try {
    24→          setLoading(true)
    25→          const serverTasks = await api.getTasks(projectId)
    26→          setTasks(serverTasks as unknown as TaskDocument[])
    27→          setLoading(false)
    28→        } catch (err) {
    29→          console.error('[useLocalTasks] Server fetch error:', err)
    30→          setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Failed to load tasks')
    31→          setLoading(false)
    32→        }
    33→      }
    34→      fetchTasks()
    35→      return
    36→    }
    37→
    38→    setLoading(true)
    39→
    40→    const subscription = db.tasks
    41→      .find({
    42→        selector: {
    43→          project_id: projectId,
    44→          _deleted: { $ne: true },
    45→        },
    46→        sort: [{ created_at: 'desc' }],
    47→      })
    48→      .$.subscribe({
    49→        next: (docs) => {
    50→          setTasks(docs.map(doc => doc.toJSON()))
    51→          setLoading(false)
    52→          setError(null)
    53→        },
    54→        error: (err) => {
    55→          console.error('[useLocalTasks] Subscription error:', err)
    56→          setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Failed to load tasks')
    57→          setLoading(false)
    58→        },
    59→      })
    60→
    61→    return () => {
    62→      subscription.unsubscribe()
    63→    }
    64→  }, [db, projectId])
    65→
    66→  /**
    67→   * Create a new task with optimistic update
    68→   */
    69→  const createTask = async (data: {
    70→    title: string
    71→    description?: string
    72→    status?: 'todo' | 'in_progress' | 'done'
    73→    swim_lane_id?: number
    74→    priority?: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high' | 'urgent'
    75→    assignee_id?: number
    76→    due_date?: string
    77→  }) => {
    78→    // Fallback to server API when RxDB is disabled
    79→    if (!db || !syncService) {
    80→      const newTask = await api.createTask(projectId, data)
    81→      setTasks(prev => [newTask as unknown as TaskDocument, ...prev])
    82→      return
    83→    }
    84→
    85→    // Generate temporary ID (will be replaced by server ID after sync)
    86→    const tempId = Date.now()
    87→
    88→    const newTask: TaskDocument = {
    89→      id: tempId,
    90→      project_id: projectId,
    91→      title: data.title,
    92→      description: data.description,
    93→      status: data.status || 'todo',
    94→      swim_lane_id: data.swim_lane_id,
    95→      priority: data.priority,
    96→      assignee_id: data.assignee_id,
    97→      due_date: data.due_date,
    98→      created_at: new Date().toISOString(),
    99→      updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   100→      _pendingSync: true,
   101→      _lastSynced: Date.now(),
   102→    }
   103→
   104→    try {
   105→      // 1. Optimistic update: Insert into local DB immediately
   106→      await db.tasks.insert(newTask)
   107→
   108→      // 2. Queue for background sync
   109→      await syncService.queueOperation('create', 'tasks', tempId, {
   110→        ...data,
   111→        project_id: projectId,
   112→      })
   113→
   114→      console.log('[useLocalTasks] Task created optimistically:', tempId)
   115→    } catch (error) {
   116→      console.error('[useLocalTasks] Failed to create task:', error)
   117→      throw error
   118→    }
   119→  }
   120→
   121→  /**
   122→   * Update a task with optimistic update
   123→   */
   124→  const updateTask = async (taskId: number, updates: UpdateTaskRequest) => {
   125→    // Fallback to server API when RxDB is disabled
   126→    if (!db || !syncService) {
   127→      const updatedTask = await api.updateTask(taskId, updates)
   128→      setTasks(prev => prev.map(t => t.id === taskId ? updatedTask as unknown as TaskDocument : t))
   129→      return
   130→    }
   131→
   132→    try {
   133→      // Find the task
   134→      const task = await db.tasks.findOne({ selector: { id: taskId } }).exec()
   135→      if (!task) throw new Error('Task not found')
   136→
   137→      // 1. Optimistic update: Update local DB immediately
   138→      await task.update({
   139→        $set: {
   140→          ...updates,
   141→          updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   142→          _pendingSync: true,
   143→        },
   144→      })
   145→
   146→      // 2. Queue for background sync
   147→      await syncService.queueOperation('update', 'tasks', taskId, updates)
   148→
   149→      console.log('[useLocalTasks] Task updated optimistically:', taskId)
   150→    } catch (error) {
   151→      console.error('[useLocalTasks] Failed to update task:', error)
   152→      throw error
   153→    }
   154→  }
   155→
   156→  /**
   157→   * Delete a task with optimistic update
   158→   */
   159→  const deleteTask = async (taskId: number) => {
   160→    if (!db || !syncService) throw new Error('Database not initialized')
   161→
   162→    try {
   163→      // Find the task
   164→      const task = await db.tasks.findOne({ selector: { id: taskId } }).exec()
   165→      if (!task) throw new Error('Task not found')
   166→
   167→      // 1. Optimistic update: Mark as deleted in local DB
   168→      await task.update({
   169→        $set: {
   170→          _deleted: true,
   171→          _pendingSync: true,
   172→        },
   173→      })
   174→
   175→      // 2. Queue for background sync
   176→      await syncService.queueOperation('delete', 'tasks', taskId, {})
   177→
   178→      console.log('[useLocalTasks] Task deleted optimistically:', taskId)
   179→    } catch (error) {
   180→      console.error('[useLocalTasks] Failed to delete task:', error)
   181→      throw error
   182→    }
   183→  }
   184→
   185→  /**
   186→   * Update task status (common operation, optimized)
   187→   */
   188→  const updateTaskStatus = async (
   189→    taskId: number,
   190→    newStatus: 'todo' | 'in_progress' | 'done'
   191→  ) => {
   192→    await updateTask(taskId, { status: newStatus })
   193→  }
   194→
   195→  return {
   196→    tasks,
   197→    loading,
   198→    error,
   199→    createTask,
   200→    updateTask,
   201→    deleteTask,
   202→    updateTaskStatus,
   203→  }
   204→}
   205→

1→/**
     2→ * RxDB Database Initialization
     3→ * Local-first database using IndexedDB
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { createRxDatabase, addRxPlugin } from 'rxdb'
     7→import { getRxStorageDexie } from 'rxdb/plugins/storage-dexie'
     8→import { RxDBDevModePlugin } from 'rxdb/plugins/dev-mode'
     9→import { wrappedValidateAjvStorage } from 'rxdb/plugins/validate-ajv'
    10→import type { RxDatabase, RxCollection } from 'rxdb'
    11→
    12→import {
    13→  userSchema,
    14→  projectSchema,
    15→  taskSchema,
    16→  sprintSchema,
    17→  tagSchema,
    18→  syncQueueSchema,
    19→  type UserDocument,
    20→  type ProjectDocument,
    21→  type TaskDocument,
    22→  type SprintDocument,
    23→  type TagDocument,
    24→  type SyncQueueDocument,
    25→} from './schema'
    26→
    27→// Add dev mode plugin in development
    28→if (import.meta.env.DEV) {
    29→  addRxPlugin(RxDBDevModePlugin)
    30→}
    31→
    32→// Database collections type
    33→export type TaskAICollections = {
    34→  users: RxCollection<UserDocument>
    35→  projects: RxCollection<ProjectDocument>
    36→  tasks: RxCollection<TaskDocument>
    37→  sprints: RxCollection<SprintDocument>
    38→  tags: RxCollection<TagDocument>
    39→  syncqueue: RxCollection<SyncQueueDocument>
    40→}
    41→
    42→export type TaskAIDatabase = RxDatabase<TaskAICollections>
    43→
    44→// Singleton database instance
    45→let dbInstance: TaskAIDatabase | null = null
    46→
    47→/**
    48→ * Initialize the RxDB database
    49→ * Creates IndexedDB with all collections
    50→ */
    51→export async function initDatabase(userId: number): Promise<TaskAIDatabase> {
    52→  // Return existing instance if already initialized
    53→  if (dbInstance) {
    54→    return dbInstance
    55→  }
    56→
    57→  try {
    58→    // Create database with AJV validator for dev mode
    59→    const db = await createRxDatabase<TaskAICollections>({
    60→      name: `taskai_${userId}`, // Separate DB per user
    61→      storage: wrappedValidateAjvStorage({ storage: getRxStorageDexie() }),
    62→      multiInstance: false, // Single tab support (can enable later)
    63→      eventReduce: true, // Performance optimization
    64→      cleanupPolicy: {
    65→        minimumDeletedTime: 1000 * 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // Keep deleted docs for 7 days
    66→        minimumCollectionAge: 1000 * 60, // Wait 1 minute before cleanup
    67→        runEach: 1000 * 60 * 5, // Run cleanup every 5 minutes
    68→        awaitReplicationsInSync: true,
    69→        waitForLeadership: true,
    70→      },
    71→    })
    72→
    73→    // Add collections
    74→    await db.addCollections({
    75→      users: {
    76→        schema: userSchema,
    77→      },
    78→      projects: {
    79→        schema: projectSchema,
    80→      },
    81→      tasks: {
    82→        schema: taskSchema,
    83→      },
    84→      sprints: {
    85→        schema: sprintSchema,
    86→      },
    87→      tags: {
    88→        schema: tagSchema,
    89→      },
    90→      syncqueue: {
    91→        schema: syncQueueSchema,
    92→      },
    93→    })
    94→
    95→    dbInstance = db
    96→
    97→    console.log('[RxDB] Database initialized successfully')
    98→
    99→    return db
   100→  } catch (error) {
   101→    console.error('[RxDB] Failed to initialize database:', error)
   102→    throw error
   103→  }
   104→}
   105→
   106→/**
   107→ * Get the current database instance
   108→ * Throws if database not initialized
   109→ */
   110→export function getDatabase(): TaskAIDatabase {
   111→  if (!dbInstance) {
   112→    throw new Error('Database not initialized. Call initDatabase() first.')
   113→  }
   114→  return dbInstance
   115→}
   116→
   117→/**
   118→ * Destroy the database instance
   119→ * Useful for logout or switching users
   120→ */
   121→export async function destroyDatabase(): Promise<void> {
   122→  if (dbInstance) {
   123→    await dbInstance.remove()
   124→    dbInstance = null
   125→    console.log('[RxDB] Database destroyed')
   126→  }
   127→}
   128→
   129→/**
   130→ * Clear all data from the database
   131→ * Keeps the database instance alive
   132→ */
   133→export async function clearDatabase(): Promise<void> {
   134→  const db = getDatabase()
   135→
   136→  await Promise.all([
   137→    db.projects.find().remove(),
   138→    db.tasks.find().remove(),
   139→    db.sprints.find().remove(),
   140→    db.tags.find().remove(),
   141→    db.syncqueue.find().remove(),
   142→  ])
   143→
   144→  console.log('[RxDB] Database cleared')
   145→}
   146→

content.thinking:
Now I see the key issue! Let me look at the `updateTask` function in `useLocalTasks.ts` more carefully.

When a task is dragged to a new swim lane, the `handleDragEnd` calls:
```typescript
await updateTask(taskId, { swim_lane_id: newSwimLaneId })
```

In `useLocalTasks.ts`, the `updateTask` function does:
```typescript
// 1. Optimistic update: Update local DB immediately
await task.update({
  $set: {
    ...updates,  // Just { swim_lane_id: newSwimLaneId }


```

Updating the local database optimistically with only the swim lane ID, while the status field remains unchanged until the sync response returns. But since the board groups by swim lane ID rather than status, the task should immediately appear in the correct lane.

Let me reconsider what the user is actually describing. Looking at the screenshot, all four tasks in the "To Do" column have status=todo and swim_lane_id=1. The user mentions these tasks are done but still showing in To Do, which suggests they were never actually marked as complete—either the drag-and-drop didn't persist or they were never updated in the first place.

```
Since the user mentions "still issues with swim lanes and status," there's likely a bug in how the sync service handles optimistic updates. I should check if those updates are getting reverted when the sync completes.
```

content.text: Let me check the sync service - this is likely where the issue is. Optimistic updates might be getting reverted.

1→/**
     2→ * Sync Service
     3→ * Handles full sync between local RxDB and remote API
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { api } from '../api'
     7→import type { TaskAIDatabase } from '../db'
     8→import type { ProjectDocument, TaskDocument, SprintDocument, TagDocument, SyncQueueDocument } from '../db/schema'
     9→
    10→export type SyncStatus = 'idle' | 'syncing' | 'synced' | 'error' | 'offline'
    11→
    12→export interface SyncState {
    13→  status: SyncStatus
    14→  lastSyncTime: number | null
    15→  error: string | null
    16→  pendingOperations: number
    17→}
    18→
    19→export class SyncService {
    20→  private db: TaskAIDatabase
    21→  private syncInterval: number | null = null
    22→  private listeners: Array<(state: SyncState) => void> = []
    23→  private state: SyncState = {
    24→    status: 'idle',
    25→    lastSyncTime: null,
    26→    error: null,
    27→    pendingOperations: 0,
    28→  }
    29→
    30→  constructor(db: TaskAIDatabase) {
    31→    this.db = db
    32→  }
    33→
    34→  /**
    35→   * Start automatic syncing at specified interval (ms)
    36→   */
    37→  startAutoSync(intervalMs: number = 30000): void {
    38→    this.stopAutoSync()
    39→
    40→    // Initial sync
    41→    this.performFullSync()
    42→
    43→    // Set up interval
    44→    this.syncInterval = window.setInterval(() => {
    45→      this.performFullSync()
    46→    }, intervalMs)
    47→
    48→    console.log(`[Sync] Auto-sync started (interval: ${intervalMs}ms)`)
    49→  }
    50→
    51→  /**
    52→   * Stop automatic syncing
    53→   */
    54→  stopAutoSync(): void {
    55→    if (this.syncInterval) {
    56→      clearInterval(this.syncInterval)
    57→      this.syncInterval = null
    58→      console.log('[Sync] Auto-sync stopped')
    59→    }
    60→  }
    61→
    62→  /**
    63→   * Subscribe to sync state changes
    64→   */
    65→  subscribe(listener: (state: SyncState) => void): () => void {
    66→    this.listeners.push(listener)
    67→    // Immediately call with current state
    68→    listener(this.state)
    69→
    70→    // Return unsubscribe function
    71→    return () => {
    72→      this.listeners = this.listeners.filter(l => l !== listener)
    73→    }
    74→  }
    75→
    76→  /**
    77→   * Update sync state and notify listeners
    78→   */
    79→  private updateState(updates: Partial<SyncState>): void {
    80→    this.state = { ...this.state, ...updates }
    81→    this.listeners.forEach(listener => listener(this.state))
    82→  }
    83→
    84→  /**
    85→   * Perform full sync with the server
    86→   * Downloads all data and replaces local data
    87→   */
    88→  async performFullSync(): Promise<void> {
    89→    // Check online status
    90→    if (!navigator.onLine) {
    91→      this.updateState({ status: 'offline', error: 'No internet connection' })
    92→      return
    93→    }
    94→
    95→    this.updateState({ status: 'syncing', error: null })
    96→
    97→    try {
    98→      // Count pending operations
    99→      const pendingCount = await this.db.syncqueue.count().exec()
   100→      this.updateState({ pendingOperations: pendingCount })
   101→
   102→      // Process pending operations first
   103→      await this.processSyncQueue()
   104→
   105→      // Pull data from server
   106→      await this.pullFromServer()
   107→
   108→      // Update state
   109→      this.updateState({
   110→        status: 'synced',
   111→        lastSyncTime: Date.now(),
   112→        error: null,
   113→        pendingOperations: 0,
   114→      })
   115→
   116→      console.log('[Sync] Full sync completed successfully')
   117→    } catch (error) {
   118→      const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown sync error'
   119→      console.error('[Sync] Full sync failed:', errorMessage)
   120→      this.updateState({
   121→        status: 'error',
   122→        error: errorMessage,
   123→      })
   124→    }
   125→  }
   126→
   127→  /**
   128→   * Pull all data from server and update local database
   129→   */
   130→  private async pullFromServer(): Promise<void> {
   131→    console.log('[Sync] Pulling data from server...')
   132→
   133→    // Fetch all data in parallel
   134→    const [projects, sprints, tags] = await Promise.all([
   135→      api.getProjects(),
   136→      api.getSprints(),
   137→      api.getTags(),
   138→    ])
   139→
   140→    // Upsert projects
   141→    for (const project of projects) {
   142→      const doc: ProjectDocument = {
   143→        id: project.id!,
   144→        owner_id: project.owner_id!,
   145→        name: project.name || '',
   146→        description: project.description || undefined,
   147→        created_at: project.created_at || new Date().toISOString(),
   148→        updated_at: project.updated_at || new Date().toISOString(),
   149→        _lastSynced: Date.now(),
   150→      }
   151→
   152→      await this.db.projects.upsert(doc)
   153→    }
   154→
   155→    // Fetch tasks for each project
   156→    for (const project of projects) {
   157→      if (project.id) {
   158→        const tasks = await api.getTasks(project.id)
   159→
   160→        for (const task of tasks) {
   161→          const doc: TaskDocument = {
   162→            id: task.id!,
   163→            project_id: task.project_id!,
   164→            title: task.title || '',
   165→            description: task.description || undefined,
   166→            status: task.status as 'todo' | 'in_progress' | 'done',
   167→            priority: task.priority as 'low' | 'medium' | 'high' | 'urgent' | undefined,
   168→            assignee_id: task.assignee_id || undefined,
   169→            sprint_id: task.sprint_id || undefined,
   170→            due_date: task.due_date || undefined,
   171→            estimated_hours: task.estimated_hours || undefined,
   172→            actual_hours: task.actual_hours || undefined,
   173→            created_at: task.created_at || new Date().toISOString(),
   174→            updated_at: task.updated_at || new Date().toISOString(),
   175→            _lastSynced: Date.now(),
   176→            _pendingSync: false,
   177→          }
   178→
   179→          await this.db.tasks.upsert(doc)
   180→        }
   181→      }
   182→    }
   183→
   184→    // Upsert sprints
   185→    for (const sprint of sprints) {
   186→      const doc: SprintDocument = {
   187→        id: sprint.id!,
   188→        user_id: sprint.user_id!,
   189→        name: sprint.name!,
   190→        goal: sprint.goal,
   191→        start_date: sprint.start_date,
   192→        end_date: sprint.end_date,
   193→        status: sprint.status as 'planned' | 'active' | 'completed',
   194→        created_at: sprint.created_at || new Date().toISOString(),
   195→        updated_at: sprint.updated_at || new Date().toISOString(),
   196→        _lastSynced: Date.now(),
   197→      }
   198→
   199→      await this.db.sprints.upsert(doc)
   200→    }
   201→
   202→    // Upsert tags
   203→    for (const tag of tags) {
   204→      const doc: TagDocument = {
   205→        id: tag.id!,
   206→        user_id: tag.user_id!,
   207→        name: tag.name!,
   208→        color: tag.color!,
   209→        created_at: tag.created_at || new Date().toISOString(),
   210→        _lastSynced: Date.now(),
   211→      }
   212→
   213→      await this.db.tags.upsert(doc)
   214→    }
   215→
   216→    console.log('[Sync] Data pulled successfully:', {
   217→      projects: projects.length,
   218→      sprints: sprints.length,
   219→      tags: tags.length,
   220→    })
   221→  }
   222→
   223→  /**
   224→   * Process pending operations in the sync queue
   225→   * Sends local changes to the server
   226→   */
   227→  private async processSyncQueue(): Promise<void> {
   228→    const queue = await this.db.syncqueue
   229→      .find()
   230→      .sort({ timestamp: 'asc' })
   231→      .exec()
   232→
   233→    console.log(`[Sync] Processing ${queue.length} pending operations`)
   234→
   235→    for (const item of queue) {
   236→      try {
   237→        await this.executeSyncOperation(item.toJSON())
   238→        await item.remove()
   239→      } catch (error) {
   240→        console.error('[Sync] Failed to process queue item:', item.id, error)
   241→
   242→        // Increment retry count
   243→        await item.update({
   244→          $inc: { retryCount: 1 },
   245→          $set: { error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' },
   246→        })
   247→
   248→        // Remove if too many retries
   249→        if (item.retryCount >= 3) {
   250→          console.error('[Sync] Max retries reached, removing item:', item.id)
   251→          await item.remove()
   252→        }
   253→      }
   254→    }
   255→  }
   256→
   257→  /**
   258→   * Execute a single sync operation
   259→   */
   260→  private async executeSyncOperation(item: SyncQueueDocument): Promise<void> {
   261→    const { operation, collection, documentId, data } = item
   262→
   263→    switch (collection) {
   264→      case 'projects':
   265→        if (operation === 'create') {
   266→          await api.createProject(data as { name: string; description?: string })
   267→        } else if (operation === 'update') {
   268→          await api.updateProject(documentId, data)
   269→        } else if (operation === 'delete') {
   270→          await api.deleteProject(documentId)
   271→        }
   272→        break
   273→
   274→      case 'tasks':
   275→        if (operation === 'create') {
   276→          await api.createTask(data.project_id as number, data as { title: string })
   277→        } else if (operation === 'update') {
   278→          await api.updateTask(documentId, data)
   279→        } else if (operation === 'delete') {
   280→          await api.deleteTask(documentId)
   281→        }
   282→        break
   283→
   284→      case 'sprints':
   285→        if (operation === 'create') {
   286→          await api.createSprint(data)
   287→        } else if (operation === 'update') {
   288→          await api.updateSprint(documentId, data)
   289→        } else if (operation === 'delete') {
   290→          await api.deleteSprint(documentId)
   291→        }
   292→        break
   293→
   294→      case 'tags':
   295→        if (operation === 'create') {
   296→          await api.createTag(data)
   297→        } else if (operation === 'update') {
   298→          await api.updateTag(documentId, data)
   299→        } else if (operation === 'delete') {
   300→          await api.deleteTag(documentId)
   301→        }
   302→        break
   303→    }
   304→  }
   305→
   306→  /**
   307→   * Queue an operation for background sync
   308→   */
   309→  async queueOperation(
   310→    operation: 'create' | 'update' | 'delete',
   311→    collection: 'projects' | 'tasks' | 'sprints' | 'tags',
   312→    documentId: number,
   313→    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
   314→    data: Record<string, any>
   315→  ): Promise<void> {
   316→    await this.db.syncqueue.insert({
   317→      id: `${collection}_${operation}_${documentId}_${Date.now()}`,
   318→      operation,
   319→      collection,
   320→      documentId,
   321→      data,
   322→      timestamp: Date.now(),
   323→      retryCount: 0,
   324→    })
   325→
   326→    // Update pending count
   327→    const pendingCount = await this.db.syncqueue.count().exec()
   328→    this.updateState({ pendingOperations: pendingCount })
   329→
   330→    console.log('[Sync] Queued operation:', { operation, collection, documentId })
   331→  }
   332→
   333→  /**
   334→   * Get current sync state
   335→   */
   336→  getState(): SyncState {
   337→    return this.state
   338→  }
   339→}
   340→

content.thinking:
Now I found the critical bug! Let me trace the full flow:

**When a task is dragged to a new swim lane:**

1. `handleDragEnd` calls `updateTask(taskId, { swim_lane_id: newSwimLaneId })`
2. `useLocalTasks.updateTask` does:
   - Optimistic update: Updates local RxDB with `{ swim_lane_id: newSwimLaneId, updated_at: ..., _pendingSync: true }`
   - Note: Only `swim_lane_id` is set locally, `status` is NOT updated
   - Queues the operation for background sync


3. Background sync sends just the `swim_lane_id` to the backend, which derives the new `status` from the lane's category and returns the full updated task
4. Then `pullFromServer` upserts this task data into the local database

The problem is in `pullFromServer` where it reconstructs the task document—I'm looking at how it's mapping the server response fields back into the local schema. I'm realizing there's a critical issue here—`swim_lane_id` isn't being included in the upsert operation, which means it could get wiped out when syncing from the server. The same goes for `task_number`, `swim_lane_name`, and `assignee_name`. In RxDB, when you upsert a document without certain fields, those fields get removed or set to undefined, so I need to make sure all the necessary fields are preserved during the sync. I found the bug—the `pullFromServer` function isn't mapping `swim_lane_id`, `task_number`, `swim_lane_name`, and `assignee_name` when it creates the TaskDocument, so when RxDB upserts it, those fields get wiped out and tasks disappear from their columns. But the screenshot shows tasks are actually displaying in columns, so maybe the board has a fallback to group by status when `swim_lane_id` is undefined.

Looking at the grouping logic, if `swim_lane_id` is undefined, tasks shouldn't appear in any column at all. Let me trace through what the server actually returns—the API response includes `swim_lane_id` and `swim_lane_name` for each task, but the document mapping only extracts id, project_id, title, description, and status.

The real issue is that the full sync runs every 30 seconds and calls `upsert` with incomplete documents missing `swim_lane_id`, `swim_lane_name`, `task_number`, `assignee_name`, and `sprint_name`. Since these fields are optional in the schema, they get set to undefined, which wipes out the swim lane assignments that were there before. The fallback path handles this differently by just replacing the entire task list.

Looking at the actual screenshot though, the tasks do have swim lane assignments visible in the columns, so maybe the real problem is that tasks #15-18 have `status=todo` in the server database when they should be marked as done, or the user tried to move them but the changes didn't persist.

The core issue is becoming clearer: `pullFromServer` is stripping out `swim_lane_id`, `task_number`, `swim_lane_name`, and `assignee_name` when it upserts documents, so every sync cycle overwrites these fields with undefined values.

But that doesn't fully explain why only tasks #15-18 are affected while the Done column tasks display correctly. If RxDB is actively running and corrupting data every 30 seconds, all tasks should be broken. This suggests RxDB might be disabled and these four tasks simply weren't synced properly in the first place. Let me check the SyncContext to see what's actually enabled.

    65→    } catch (error) {
    66→      console.error('[SyncContext] Failed to initialize database:', error)
    67→      setSyncState({
    68→        status: 'error',
    69→        lastSyncTime: null,
    70→        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Database initialization failed',
    71→        pendingOperations: 0,
    72→      })
    73→    }
    74→  }
    75→
    76→  // Destroy database when user logs out
    77→  const destroySync = async () => {
    78→    if (syncService) {
    79→      syncService.stopAutoSync()
    80→    }
    81→
    82→    if (db) {

    84→      setDb(null)
    85→      setSyncService(null)

    91→        pendingOperations: 0,

    93→      console.log('[SyncContext] Database destroyed')
    94→    }
    95→  }
    96→
    97→  // Manual sync trigger
    98→  const triggerSync = async () => {
    99→    if (syncService) {
   100→      await syncService.performFullSync()
   101→    }
   102→  }
   103→
   104→  // Auto-initialize when user logs in
   105→  useEffect(() => {
   106→    if (user && !isInitialized) {
   107→      initializeSync()
   108→    } else if (!user && isInitialized) {
   109→      destroySync()
   110→    }
   111→  }, [user?.id])
   112→
   113→  // Cleanup on unmount
   114→  useEffect(() => {
   115→    return () => {

   131→
   132→  return <SyncContext.Provider value={value}>{children}</SyncContext.Provider>
   133→}
   134→
   135→export function useSync() {
